title: ops: finish Robust.Cdn integration — CI publish pipeline + cleanup
description:
## Context
vs-3mv (CLOSED 2026-04-13) stood up Robust.Cdn as a docker-compose
service alongside observability at `https://ss14.zig.computer/cdn/`,
added `external/robust-cdn/` submodule + `ops/robust-cdn/` configs, and
switched the watchdog's `UpdateType` from `Local` to `Manifest`
pointing at our CDN's manifest URL.

**What's left is the publishing half:** CI-driven build→publish on
main pushes, plus dead-code cleanup. Leaving these items parked
inside vs-3mv's closed task list was an anti-pattern (closed-bead-
with-undone-work). This bead is the proper follow-up that retires
that gap.

## Current state (verified 2026-04-17)
- ✓ `external/robust-cdn/` submodule present
- ✓ `ops/robust-cdn/` configs (`appsettings.Production.json`)
- ✓ CDN service live: `https://ss14.zig.computer/cdn/fork/vacation-station` → 200
- ✓ Watchdog on `UpdateType: Manifest` pointing at our CDN
- ✗ No CI publishing pipeline writes to our CDN
- ✗ `instances/vacation-station/binaries/` drop-in hack still present (dead code under Manifest mode)
- ✗ `docs/upstream-sync.md` CDN section not updated

## Scope — agent work

### 1. Repoint publish workflows at our CDN
Currently `.github/workflows/publish.yml` + `publish-testing.yml`
default to `wizards.cdn.spacestation14.com` via defaults in
`Tools/publish_multi_request.py`. Repoint:

- **`Tools/publish_multi_request.py`**:
  - `ROBUST_CDN_URL = "https://ss14.zig.computer/cdn/"`
  - `FORK_ID = "vacation-station"` (default; workflow flag still supported)
- **`.github/workflows/publish-testing.yml`**:
  - Uncomment the `schedule: cron` block (my 2026-04-16 `:wrench:`
    commit that disabled it was a stepping stone to this)
  - Update the `--fork-id` default or drop the flag
- **`.github/workflows/publish.yml`**:
  - Decide: do we want this on push to main, or keep it
    workflow_dispatch-only for now? Recommend: push-to-main once
    manual test passes.
- Annotate changed lines with `# VS -` per our upstream-modifications
  convention (so future upstream-sw syncs don't silently clobber).

### 2. Verify CDN publish auth
Check `ops/robust-cdn/appsettings.Production.json` for the auth mechanism
(Robust.Cdn supports bearer tokens via `AuthTokens` or similar). If
a token slot exists but is empty, generate one. If not, add a
section.

### 3. Remove the `binaries/` drop-in hack
`instances/vacation-station/binaries/` becomes dead code the moment
Manifest mode works end-to-end. After step 4 validates, delete the
dir + document the removal.

### 4. Update docs
`docs/upstream-sync.md` — flip the CDN row from "deployed (deferred
integration)" to "fully integrated, CI-driven". Note the VS14
fork-id + manifest URL.

## Scope — human work (mixed bead, clearly called out)

### H1. Generate + register the publish token
- Mint a `PUBLISH_TOKEN` on our CDN instance (configure in
  `ops/robust-cdn/appsettings.Production.json`, restart the service).
- Register it as a **GitHub Actions secret** named `PUBLISH_TOKEN`
  on `azigler/vacation-station-14`.
- Agent cannot do this — needs repo-admin + server-admin access.

### H2. End-to-end test
- After agent lands steps 1–4, manually trigger the publish-testing
  workflow (`gh workflow run publish-testing.yml`).
- Verify: CI runs green → CDN's manifest reports a new version →
  watchdog picks it up on next restart cycle → a fresh launcher
  client downloads from the CDN.
- If anything breaks, loop back to agent for fixes.

### H3. Launch readiness signoff
- Confirm the CDN flow before hub advertisement (vs-17n) — we don't
  want cold inbound launcher traffic with the Local/Manifest
  transition half-wired.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `Tools/publish_multi_request.py` defaults point at our CDN
- [ ] `publish-testing.yml` cron re-enabled, annotated `# VS -`
- [ ] `publish.yml` trigger decided + documented
- [ ] CDN auth configured; `PUBLISH_TOKEN` registered as repo secret (human)
- [ ] End-to-end test passes: CI merge → CDN → watchdog → client (human)
- [ ] `instances/vacation-station/binaries/` removed
- [ ] `docs/upstream-sync.md` CDN row updated

## Dependencies
- vs-3mv (CLOSED) — the prior half of this work; spec still valid
- Blocks vs-17n (hub advertise) — CDN must be verified before
  cold launcher traffic

## Priority
P2 under vs-2f8 (production ops hardening). Pre-launch priority;
should land before hub advertisement.

## Out of scope
- Automated client-browser rollout testing (humans connect to verify)
- Replacing our CDN host with a managed one — current setup is fine

title: ops: deploy SS14.Changelog when contributor/launch trigger fires
description:
## Context
vs-3v4 (CLOSED 2026-04-12) specified the SS14.Changelog webhook
service deployment in full detail but did none of the work —
closed prematurely as "scoped, will execute when triggered." This
bead is the proper companion that executes that spec when the
trigger fires. Matches the pattern established by vs-fxx (CDN
finish): closed-bead-with-undone-work is replaced with a live
follow-up.

## What SS14.Changelog does (1-paragraph recap)
ASP.NET Core webhook receiver. Watches PR merges on our repo,
scrapes `:cl:` blocks from PR descriptions, auto-commits them
into `Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml`. Game server reads that
file at startup and shows players a "what's new since you last
connected" panel in-game.

MIT-licensed, Wizden-maintained. Deploy-vanilla-defer-fork pattern
same as SS14.Admin, MapServer, cookbook.

## Trigger to unpark
Deploy when **any one** of the following is true:
- First external contributor opens a PR against VS14 (their `:cl:`
  block should reach players automatically — good community signal)
- Pre-launch readiness sweep is green on everything else and this
  is the last polish item
- Manual `:cl:` curation of `Changelog.yml` becomes annoying

Until a trigger fires, this bead stays deferred. No CI cost, no
infrastructure cost.

## Scope (execute when triggered)
Full spec lives in **vs-3v4** (closed). Summary:

### Agent work
1. Add `external/ss14-changelog/` submodule (upstream:
   `space-wizards/SS14.Changelog`) — name matches vs-hxx naming
   consistency decision
2. Write `ops/ss14-changelog/` deployment:
   - `docker-compose.yml` (builds from submodule, binds
     `127.0.0.1:<port>`, env-loaded secrets)
   - `appsettings.Production.yml.example` (template; real config
     env-injected)
   - `Caddyfile.block` routing `hooks.<domain>/ss14-changelog/` to
     the local port
3. Verify `Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml` exists + is parseable
4. Update `docs/upstream-sync.md` with the new row
5. Update `CONTRIBUTING.md` documenting the `:cl:` block expectation
   in PR descriptions (our `/commit` skill already emits it in
   commit messages; PR descriptions need the same)
6. Cross-ref `/commit` skill to the PR-description `:cl:` workflow
7. License audit row in LEGAL.md (MIT, attribution preserved)

### Human work
1. **GitHub deploy key**: register a write-access deploy key on
   `azigler/vacation-station-14` so the service can push to
   `Changelog.yml`. Store the private half at
   `/etc/vacation-station/changelog-deploy-key` with tight perms.
2. **Webhook registration**: register a webhook on the repo pointing
   at `hooks.<domain>/ss14-changelog/hook` with `Pull requests` +
   `Pushes` events. Shared secret matches `GitHub:WebhookSecret`
   in appsettings.
3. **Committer identity**: decide whether to create a
   `ss14-changelog-bot` GitHub account or reuse an existing one.
4. **First-run test**: open a test PR with a `:cl:` block in its
   body, merge it, confirm webhook fires (Caddy access log),
   confirm service commits the update, confirm in-game changelog
   panel picks it up on next server restart.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `external/ss14-changelog/` submodule added
- [ ] `ops/ss14-changelog/` deployment configs committed
- [ ] Deploy key registered (human)
- [ ] Webhook registered (human)
- [ ] docker-compose stack running under systemd
- [ ] Caddy routing `hooks` subdomain
- [ ] Test PR merge triggers `Changelog.yml` update
- [ ] In-game changelog panel displays the new entry
- [ ] `CONTRIBUTING.md` documents the `:cl:` block expectation for PRs
- [ ] `/commit` skill cross-refs the PR-description `:cl:` workflow
- [ ] `docs/upstream-sync.md` + LEGAL.md updated

## Dependencies
- vs-3v4 (CLOSED) — the spec reference; this bead executes it
- vs-2y8 (CLOSED, Caddy fronts watchdog) — webhook needs HTTPS

## Priority
P3 under vs-2f8 (production ops hardening). Deferred, trigger-based.

## Out of scope
- Per-category changelog filtering (upstream supports; we can
  enable later)
- Migration of historical commit `:cl:` blocks into `Changelog.yml`
  (start fresh)
- Discord cross-post of changelog entries — pairs with vs-2l2's
  Discord integration; file separate bead when needed

## Notes
Post-Flavor-A: changelog file is at SS14 default
`Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml`, NOT Delta-V's
`DeltaVChangelog.yml`. Set `ChangelogFilename` accordingly.

title: refactor: normalize external/ + ops/ dir names (robust-cdn vs cdn)
description:
## Context
`external/` and `ops/` dir names should map 1:1 across the two
roots for the same upstream repo. Currently one pair is out of
sync:

| upstream | `external/` | `ops/` |
|---|---|---|
| `space-wizards/Robust.Cdn` | `robust-cdn` | `cdn` ← mismatch |

Every other service pair already matches. Fixing this one ensures
future service adoptions follow a mechanical rule (**lowercase
kebab of upstream repo name**) without ambiguity.

## Decision
Rename `ops/cdn/` → `ops/robust-cdn/`. Choose `external/` side's
naming as authoritative since it directly mirrors the upstream
repo (`Robust.Cdn` → `robust-cdn`).

## Scope — agent work
1. `git mv ops/cdn ops/robust-cdn`
2. Update any reference to `ops/cdn/` across the repo:
   - `docs/upstream-sync.md`
   - `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` if present
   - `CLAUDE.md` if the path is mentioned
   - `ops/nginx/` vhosts if any route references the config path
   - any install scripts or systemd units that reference the path
   - `.github/workflows/` — no current hit but double-check
3. Verify `docker compose -f ops/robust-cdn/docker-compose.yml config`
   still parses post-move
4. Verify CDN still reachable at
   `https://ss14.zig.computer/cdn/fork/vacation-station` (the URL
   path is unaffected — that's nginx/caddy-side routing; only the
   dir name changes)
5. Commit with the `:truck:` gitmoji (rename/move convention)

## Convention to codify in CLAUDE.md
Add a short section under "Code Conventions" documenting:
> `external/<name>/` + `ops/<name>/` MUST use the same `<name>`,
> which is the lowercase kebab of the upstream repo name
> (`Robust.Cdn` → `robust-cdn`). Prefix-stripping is allowed
> when redundant (`SS14.MapServer` → `mapserver`) as long as
> both roots agree.

## Dispatch
This is a well-scoped mechanical rename, appropriate for a worktree
subagent. Orchestrator dispatches per CLAUDE.md subagent conventions.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `ops/cdn/` renamed to `ops/robust-cdn/`
- [ ] Every repo reference to `ops/cdn/` updated
- [ ] CDN service still serves content at same public URL
- [ ] CLAUDE.md updated with the naming convention
- [ ] `docs/upstream-sync.md` updated

## Priority
P3 under vs-2f8 (prod ops hardening) — cosmetic cleanup, not
blocking anything. Good first subagent task.

## Out of scope
- Renaming `external/robust-cdn/` (we're picking this as the
  authoritative form — no change needed)
- Touching watchdog update URLs (manifest URL is nginx/caddy-side,
  not filesystem-coupled)
close_reason: Done. Renamed ops/cdn/ → ops/robust-cdn/, updated ops/observability/docker-compose.yml volume mount path, codified the external/+ops naming rule in CLAUDE.md. Subagent commit 88b78cc09b merged via fast-forward. Public URL at ss14.zig.computer/cdn/ unaffected (Caddy-routed, not filesystem-coupled). Docker volume names + container names intentionally not renamed to avoid breaking running prod state.

title: ops: fix Docker cleanup — observability container_name + admin healthcheck
description:
## Context
Two cosmetic Docker-side inconsistencies surfaced during the
2026-04-17 runtime topology audit. Fix both in one pass.

### Issue 1 — Observability container names missing
`ops/observability/docker-compose.yml` sets `container_name:` on
`mapserver:` and `cdn:` but skips it on `prometheus:`, `loki:`, and
`grafana:`. Result: those three get Docker Compose's default
`<project>-<service>-<replica#>` naming:

```
vs14-observability-grafana-1
vs14-observability-loki-1
vs14-observability-prometheus-1
```

Wanted: match the `vs14-<service>` shape the rest of the stack uses.

### Issue 2 — Admin healthcheck uses wget which isn't in the image
`vs14-ss14-admin` container has an image-level `HEALTHCHECK`
configured to run `wget <url>`, but the image doesn't include
`wget`. Result: every 30s the healthcheck fails with
`/bin/sh: 1: wget: not found`, exit 1, and Docker reports the
container as `unhealthy` even though the app is fully working
(HTTP 200, successful OIDC auth, admin UI responsive).

## Scope — agent work

### Part 1: Observability container names
1. Edit `ops/observability/docker-compose.yml` — add
   `container_name:` to the `prometheus:`, `loki:`, and `grafana:`
   service blocks. Match the existing pattern:
   ```yaml
   prometheus:
     container_name: vs14-prometheus
     ...
   loki:
     container_name: vs14-loki
     ...
   grafana:
     container_name: vs14-grafana
     ...
   ```

2. Apply the change to the running stack with a **targeted recreate**
   — do NOT use `docker compose down` (would take CDN + mapserver
   down too). The compose project is running from
   `/opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml`,
   not the repo clone — copy the edited file there first, then run:
   ```bash
   sudo cp /home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml \
           /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml
   cd /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability
   sudo docker compose up -d --force-recreate prometheus loki grafana
   ```

3. Verify the new names:
   ```bash
   docker ps --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -E 'vs14-(prometheus|loki|grafana)$'
   ```
   Should show exactly three rows, no `-1` suffix.

4. Verify data persistence:
   - Grafana: `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:3200/api/health`
     → expect 200
   - Prometheus: `curl -sS 'http://127.0.0.1:9090/api/v1/query?query=up' | grep -oE '"value":\[[0-9.]+' | head -1`
     → should show a recent scrape timestamp
   - Loki: `curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' http://127.0.0.1:3100/ready`
     → expect 200

### Part 2: Admin healthcheck fix
Preferred: **override the healthcheck in compose** so we don't
have to rebuild the image. In `ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`,
add under the `ss14-admin:` service:

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD-SHELL", "curl -fsS http://localhost:8080/admin/ || exit 1"]
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 30s
```

(Verify the actual port the app listens on inside the container —
might be 80 or 8080 depending on the Dockerfile. Check
`docker inspect vs14-ss14-admin` → `Config.ExposedPorts` and
`Config.Env` for `ASPNETCORE_URLS`. Also verify `curl` IS in the
image before committing to this — if neither `curl` nor `wget`
is present, fall back to disabling the healthcheck with
`test: ["NONE"]` and note the reason in a comment.)

Apply:
```bash
cd /home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/ops/ss14-admin
sudo docker compose up -d
```

Admin goes down for ~5s during the recreate — admin users get a
single 502 then it reconnects. Acceptable at pre-launch scale.

Verify:
```bash
sleep 60  # let the new healthcheck settle
docker inspect vs14-ss14-admin --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'
# expect: healthy
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}\n' https://ss14.zig.computer/admin/
# expect: 200
```

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `ops/observability/docker-compose.yml` adds `container_name:` to prometheus/loki/grafana
- [ ] `/opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml` mirrors the repo edit (deployed copy)
- [ ] Running containers rename to `vs14-prometheus`, `vs14-loki`, `vs14-grafana` (no `-1` suffix)
- [ ] Data persistence verified: Grafana dashboards still present, Prometheus metrics still scraping, Loki ingesting logs
- [ ] `ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml` has working healthcheck override
- [ ] `docker inspect vs14-ss14-admin` reports `healthy` (not `unhealthy`) after ~2 min
- [ ] `https://ss14.zig.computer/admin/` still returns 200 with auth
- [ ] Committed with `:wrench:` gitmoji (config fix convention)

## Priority
P3 under vs-2f8 — cosmetic cleanup, not blocking anything, but
quality-of-ops win. Noise reduction in `docker ps` output + accurate
health signal on the admin container.

## Out of scope
- Rebuilding the ss14-admin image (we're overriding at compose-level)
- Moving the /opt/vacation-station/ops/ copy issue (separate
  deployment-discipline bead; not filing here)
- Adding healthchecks to services that don't have them (observability
  stack doesn't need ours — Prometheus self-monitors)
close_reason: Done. Observability containers renamed to vs14-prometheus/vs14-loki/vs14-grafana (no -1 suffix). Admin healthcheck disabled via test: [NONE] + comment (image lacked both curl AND wget — documented fallback path per bead guardrail 3). All verifications pass: docker ps clean, no unhealthy tags, HTTPS 200 on admin, data persistence confirmed on prometheus/loki/grafana volumes. Subagent commit e7ccb2f54d merged fast-forward.

title: ops: codify GitOps deploy discipline + normalize admin path + healthcheck
description:
## Context
The 2026-04-17 runtime audit surfaced four related issues that all
sit at the "prod deploy discipline" seam:

1. **No documented GitOps flow for `/opt` clone.** The `/services`
   skill (vs-1cb) documents the dev/prod stack split but doesn't
   say how config changes propagate from `/home/ubuntu/...` (where
   commits happen) to `/opt/vacation-station/` (where services
   read configs from). Past subagents "helped" by direct-editing
   both clones simultaneously ("belt-and-suspenders") which caused
   a merge conflict during vs-2f8.4 cleanup because the two clones
   diverged pre-merge.

2. **Admin compose project runs from `/home`, not `/opt`.** Every
   other long-lived service (observability stack, all systemd
   build units) runs from `/opt/vacation-station/`. Admin is the
   only outlier. Noted in vs-2f8.4's deviation report.

3. **Admin healthcheck is `test: ["NONE"]`.** vs-2f8.4 fell back
   to disabling the healthcheck because the upstream image lacks
   both `curl` and `wget`. We can do better with a bash TCP probe
   (bash IS present in the image — verified 2026-04-17).

4. **Orient skill wording can be tightened** (verification only —
   current content already uses a glob + forbids Explore subagent;
   reads fine, but phrasing could be unambiguous).

## Scope — agent work

### Part 1: `/services` skill update
Add a new section, **"Deploying changes"**, documenting the
canonical GitOps flow:

```
1. Edit configs in the DEV clone: /home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/
2. Commit + push to origin
3. On the PROD clone: cd /opt/vacation-station && git pull --rebase
   (include `git submodule update --init --recursive` if submodule
   changes landed)
4. Apply to the live system:
   - docker compose service: `cd /opt/vacation-station/ops/<name>
     && docker compose up -d` (recreates only changed containers)
   - systemd service: `sudo systemctl restart <unit>.service`
5. Verify: `docker ps` / `systemctl status` / targeted HTTP probe

NEVER direct-edit files under /opt/vacation-station/. The clone
there is a read-only mirror maintained via git pull. If you find
yourself wanting to touch /opt/ directly, you almost certainly
want to edit /home/, commit, push, and pull instead.
```

Add a second subsection, **"Subagents doing ops work"**, with a
one-liner rule: subagents dispatched for ops tasks must edit
ONLY their worktree (for the commit) and the LIVE deploy location
(for the running service). Do NOT sync the main `/home/` clone
directly — the orchestrator's merge handles that. Direct main-clone
edits cause merge conflicts (see vs-2f8.4 post-mortem).

### Part 2: CLAUDE.md pointer
Add a short section **`## Production Deploy Discipline`** under
the existing "Dev Environment" section. Three sentences max:

> Prod runs from a second repo clone at `/opt/vacation-station/`
> maintained via `git pull` against origin — never direct-edit
> files there. Dev/commit work happens in `/home/ubuntu/vacation-
> station-14/`. See `.claude/skills/services/SKILL.md` "Deploying
> changes" for the full flow.

### Part 3: Orient skill tightening (verification + phrasing)
Current file at `.claude/skills/orient/SKILL.md` already uses the
`.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` glob and forbids Explore subagent use.
No substantive change — just tighten the phrasing. Suggested
rewrite for lines 12–18:

```markdown
1. `CLAUDE.md` — project conventions
2. **Every skill under `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` — read all of
   them yourself, DIRECTLY.** Do NOT dispatch the `Explore`
   subagent (or any subagent) to summarize. Load each skill's
   content into this conversation's context directly — summaries
   lose the load-bearing details. Skills are short; read them all.
```

Makes the intent "read all your skills into this session's
context, yourself" unambiguous.

### Part 4: Admin compose project — move to `/opt`
Today:
- `docker compose ls` shows admin project at
  `/home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`
- Observability runs from `/opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/`
- The admin compose file's `env_file:` references `.env` (relative
  path to the compose dir) AND `/etc/vacation-station/admin-oauth.env`
  (absolute)

To normalize, the relative `.env` becomes a problem — it currently
lives at `/home/.../ops/ss14-admin/.env` and would need to exist at
`/opt/.../ops/ss14-admin/.env` for the compose to run from /opt.

**Chosen approach (cleanest):** move `.env` OUT of the compose
directory entirely, into `/etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env`
(same family as the existing `admin-oauth.env` system-level env
file). Update the compose file's `env_file:` to reference both
as absolute paths. Makes the compose file clone-agnostic — works
from /home OR /opt without any relative-path coupling.

Steps:
1. `sudo mkdir -p /etc/vacation-station && sudo chmod 755 /etc/vacation-station`
2. `sudo cp /home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/ops/ss14-admin/.env /etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env`
3. `sudo chown root:ss14 /etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env && sudo chmod 640 /etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env`
   (verify `ss14` group exists; use `root:root 640` if not — admin runs
   as root anyway under docker)
4. Edit `ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`: change the `.env` line in
   `env_file:` to `/etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env`. Keep both entries
   absolute.
5. Update `ops/ss14-admin/.env.example` accordingly (or delete it and
   add an `/etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env.example` at the repo root's
   `ops/ss14-admin/`).
6. Add a note in `ops/ss14-admin/README.md` (create if absent) documenting
   the env-file location + bootstrap.
7. `cd /home/ubuntu/vacation-station-14/ops/ss14-admin && sudo docker compose down`
8. `cd /opt/vacation-station && git pull --rebase` (to pick up the
   compose + docs changes after the subagent commits — this step
   happens AFTER commit, see verification below)
9. `cd /opt/vacation-station/ops/ss14-admin && sudo docker compose up -d`
10. Verify with `docker compose ls` — project path should now be
    `/opt/vacation-station/ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`

### Part 5: Admin healthcheck — bash TCP probe
In `ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`, replace the current
`healthcheck: { test: ["NONE"] }` block with:

```yaml
healthcheck:
  test: ["CMD-SHELL", "bash -c 'exec 3<>/dev/tcp/127.0.0.1/5427' 2>/dev/null"]
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 5s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 30s
```

The container uses `network_mode: host`, so `127.0.0.1:5427` is
where the ASP.NET Core app listens (per `ASPNETCORE_URLS:
http://127.0.0.1:5427` in the `environment:` block). Bash's
`/dev/tcp/host/port` opens a TCP socket — if it connects, exit 0
(healthy); if refused, exit 1 (unhealthy).

Remove the `# VS - image lacks curl + wget;` comment that preceded
the NONE block (no longer accurate).

## Acceptance criteria

### Docs
- [ ] `/services` skill has new "Deploying changes" section with
      the 5-step GitOps flow + the "NEVER direct-edit /opt" rule
- [ ] `/services` skill has "Subagents doing ops work" subsection
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` has `## Production Deploy Discipline` section
      pointing at the skill
- [ ] `.claude/skills/orient/SKILL.md` Step 1 wording tightened per Part 3

### Admin deploy
- [ ] `/etc/vacation-station/admin-db.env` exists with 640 perms
- [ ] `ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml` references only absolute
      env_file paths
- [ ] `docker compose ls` shows admin project at
      `/opt/vacation-station/ops/ss14-admin/docker-compose.yml`
      (not the /home path)
- [ ] `ops/ss14-admin/.env` removed from repo dir (replaced by system-level)
- [ ] Old /home-based admin stack is `down`; only /opt-based stack running

### Admin healthcheck
- [ ] Compose file has bash TCP probe healthcheck, `test: ["NONE"]` gone
- [ ] After ~90s, `docker inspect vs14-ss14-admin --format '{{.State.Health.Status}}'`
      returns `healthy`
- [ ] `docker ps` shows admin container without the `(unhealthy)` tag

### End-to-end
- [ ] `https://ss14.zig.computer/admin/` returns 200 with auth
- [ ] OIDC sign-in still works (throwaway admin test or confirm existing
      session persists — sessions live in db, so they survive container
      recreate)

## Priority
P3 under vs-2f8 — documentation + ops-discipline cleanup. Not
blocking anything but reduces future friction for every ops task
going forward.

## Out of scope
- Consolidating admin + observability into a single compose file
  (separate decision; non-trivial due to host vs bridge networking)
- Migrating systemd build units away from `/opt` path references
  (current paths are correct for the dual-clone pattern we're
  codifying — nothing to change)
- Rewriting subagent type definitions to encode the GitOps rule
  (subagent types are generic; rule belongs in skill + per-task
  prompt)

## Dispatch
Well-scoped for a worktree subagent. Orchestrator dispatches per
CLAUDE.md subagent conventions, includes in the task prompt an
explicit "DO NOT belt-and-suspenders sync to the main clone"
instruction (derived from Part 1's subagent rule).

title: fix: silence grafana paths.plugins dir-missing log noise
description:
## Context
After vs-2yf fixed the grafana preinstall plugin errors, two non-fatal log lines remain when booting `nix run .#dev-services`:
```
Failed to get renderer plugin sources
Failed to load external plugins
```
These originate from grafana's `paths.plugins` setting pointing at a non-existent directory when `declarativePlugins` is null. Non-blocking — grafana boots and serves fine — just log noise.

## Task
Silence the two log lines in the dev services-flake grafana config.

Approach options (pick whichever the services-flake grafana module actually supports):
- **A**: set `services.grafana.graf1.extraConf.paths.plugins` to a writable dev-local path (e.g. `./.data/grafana/plugins`) so grafana finds an empty-but-present dir
- **B**: set `services.grafana.graf1.declarativePlugins = [ ]` explicitly instead of null so the module builds a proper empty plugins dir in the nix store
- **C**: override the grafana.ini `paths.plugins` to an empty string or `/dev/null`-ish path that suppresses the scan (verify with grafana 12.3 behavior)

Whatever path you take, verify with the smoke test below.

## Validation
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` to nix commands.

```
rm -rf .data
nix run .#dev-services -- --tui=false > /tmp/vs14-stack.log 2>&1 &
sleep 60
grep -E "Failed to (get renderer|load external) plugin" /tmp/vs14-stack.log | wc -l   # expect 0
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "grafana: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:3000/api/health    # expect 200
pkill -f process-compose; rm -rf .data
```

Also confirm the other three services still work (prom/loki/pg) — don't regress vs-2yf.

## Out of scope
- Don't touch any service except grafana
- Don't modify prod observability
- Don't add real plugins to the dev stack

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep` of the two specific "Failed to ..." lines returns 0 matches after 60s boot
- [ ] Grafana health still 200
- [ ] Prometheus, Loki, Postgres still healthy (vs-2yf validation unchanged)
- [ ] Single commit, `:bug:` gitmoji, `Bead: vs-2kv` trailer

## Notes
If none of the approaches silence the warnings cleanly, document what you tried and close this as "acceptable log noise" with a commented note in flake.nix. The warnings are harmless — do not bend the flake into a pretzel over them.

title: ops: enable replay auto-record + retention + training-data pipeline
description:
## Context
SS14 ships per-round replay recording: each round can be captured as a zip
containing per-tick state snapshots + checkpoints + metadata (map, gamemode,
players, round id, engine version). Playback is deterministic on the same
engine version.

**Current state** (VS14 day-1):
- `replay.server_recording_enabled = true` (engine default; recording
  machinery is loaded)
- `replay.auto_record = false` (content default; no per-round auto-start)
- Default location `<instance>/data/replays/<template>.zip`
- No retention policy, no extraction pipeline, no cold storage

User goal: **collect every round as training data for an eventual ML
pipeline.** Treat replays as research assets, not transient debug artifacts.

## Scope

### Phase 1 — enable auto-record with safe defaults (committable today)
1. Add `[replay]` block to `instances/vacation-station/config.toml.example`:
   ```toml
   [replay]
   auto_record = true
   # Nested path for sensible ls / sharding / retention globbing
   auto_record_name = "{year}/{month}/round_{round}-{day}T{hour}{minute}Z.zip"
   # Optional staging dir — on fast local disk, moved to final location
   # only after round ends. Useful if final dir is on a slow NFS mount.
   # auto_record_temp_dir = "/var/tmp/vs14-replays-staging"
   record_admin_chat = false  # explicit re-assert upstream default; keeps
                              # admin channel out of training data
   ```
   Apply the same block to the LIVE `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml`.

2. Document caps (inherited from Robust defaults — only override with
   intent):
   - `replay.max_compressed_size = 512MB`
   - `replay.max_uncompressed_size = 1GB`
   - Recording auto-stops when either cap hits — partial replay is kept.

3. Disk budget math: at 1–3 GB/day (8–12 rounds) on the current 174 GB free
   partition, unrotated replays fill disk in 2–6 months. Retention pipeline
   (Phase 2) prevents that.

### Phase 2 — retention + rotation (local)
Mirror the pg_dump pattern in `ops/postgres/backup.sh`:

4. **`ops/replays/rotate.sh`** — keep last N days on local disk, compress
   older metadata-only sidecars, delete raw zips past retention.
   - Daily keep: 14 days of raw zips
   - Metadata-only keep: 180 days (extract `replay_final.yml` + round stats
     before deleting the zip; the yml is tiny, lets us index what we had)
   - Configurable via env vars so it generalizes.

5. **`ops/replays/ss14-replay-rotate.service` + `.timer`** — daily systemd
   timer (like `ss14-backup`), runs as `ss14` user, ReadWritePaths scoped.

6. Update `docs/OPERATIONS.md` Replays section.

### Phase 3 — cold-storage archive (S3-compatible)
7. **`ops/replays/archive.sh`** — before local rotate deletes, upload raw
   zips to S3/Backblaze B2/Hetzner Object Storage. Structure:
   ```
   s3://vs14-replays/
     year=2026/month=04/day=12/round_123-...zip
     year=2026/month=04/day=12/round_123-...metadata.json
   ```
   Partitioned so ML training can filter by date / map / gamemode cheaply.

8. Credentials: S3 access keys via
   `/etc/vacation-station/replay-archive.env` (not in repo, not in git),
   loaded by systemd `EnvironmentFile=`.

9. Upload verification: after successful upload, write a `.uploaded`
   marker; rotate only deletes local zips that have a marker. Prevents
   silent data loss on transient S3 failures.

### Phase 4 — training-data catalog
10. **`ops/replays/index.py`** (or Rust, matching repo conventions) — walk
    the local + S3 replay tree, parse each replay's `replay_final.yml`,
    emit a single `index.parquet` / `index.jsonl` with columns:
    - `round_id`, `timestamp_utc`, `map`, `gamemode`, `player_count`,
      `round_duration_s`, `engine_version`, `content_hash`, `s3_key`,
      `file_size_bytes`
11. Rebuild index on each replay completion + nightly.
12. Downstream consumers (not built here) can query this to pick training
    subsets (e.g. "all SecretSanta rounds on saltern with >30 players from
    2026-Q2").

### Phase 5 — PII + content policy (policy doc, not code)
Before any external distribution of replays:
13. Replays bundle the chat stream EXCEPT admin chat (we already set
    `record_admin_chat = false`).
14. Regular player chat still includes Wizards Den usernames. Some
    jurisdictions treat usernames as PII.
15. **Decide**: private-only training (no publish) needs no scrubbing.
    Public-publish training needs a chat-scrub pass before upload OR
    publicly-usable-variant of each replay.
16. Document the chosen policy in `docs/OPERATIONS.md` and, if
    public-publish, build a `scripts/scrub_replay.py` that rewrites chat
    frames and emits a `scrubbed` variant.

### Phase 6 — engine version pinning for playback
17. Replay format is tied to a specific Robust engine version + content
    assembly hash. `replay_final.yml` records these; training tooling
    MUST pin to a matching runtime. Document this in the catalog schema.
18. When we bump engine via upstream-sync, add a test that plays back at
    least one recent replay to confirm format compatibility — catches
    breaking engine changes before they strand an archive.

## Dependencies
- **vs-3ty done** (postgres running) — no direct link, but shares the
  timer+service pattern.
- **vs-25k done** (obs stack) — Phase 2 rotation should export a
  Prometheus metric for zips archived / zips pruned / backlog depth so
  Grafana can alert on runaway replay growth.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Phase 1 (must-have, blocks landing):**
- [ ] `[replay]` block in `config.toml.example` with `auto_record = true`
- [ ] Live config.toml patched; game server restarted; verified a round
      produced a zip at
      `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/data/replays/…`
- [ ] `docs/OPERATIONS.md` has a Replays section covering the defaults,
      where they land, and how to manually trigger via admin commands
- [ ] Disk-growth monitoring added (at minimum: prometheus query or
      doc-level watch item)

**Phase 2 (follow-up, same bead):**
- [ ] `ops/replays/rotate.sh` + systemd timer running daily
- [ ] 14d local raw / 180d metadata retention verified on real data
- [ ] Prometheus metric for replay-dir size

**Phase 3 (follow-up, may split to separate bead):**
- [ ] S3 upload script with credential loading from env file
- [ ] `.uploaded` marker protocol preventing pre-upload deletes

**Phases 4–6**: tracked separately (future beads) once Phase 1–3 land.

## Out of scope for this bead
- The ML training code itself — this bead is about CAPTURING training
  data correctly and durably. Model training is a separate project
- Replay viewer UX improvements
- Changing Robust's replay format or bumping caps

## Notes
- Admin manual commands exist (`replaystart`, `replaystop`) for ad-hoc
  captures outside the auto-record flow.
- The `auto_record_name` template supports subdir splits — the
  `{year}/{month}/` partition scheme above makes s3 rotation globs and
  ML-training subset queries dramatically easier than a flat dir.
- Format stability is the real long-term risk. Pin engine versions in the
  catalog and don't delete replays whose engine version is no longer
  supported without archiving them first.
- When VS14 diverges far from upstream Robust, we may need to take over
  replay format maintenance ourselves. Not today, but be aware.

title: ops: bootstrap discord server + watchdog integration + community bot
description:
## Context
A SS14 community server needs a Discord. Discord is where:
- Rules are posted + acknowledged
- Players ask admins for help when they're not in-game
- Crash alerts + restart notifications flow from the watchdog
- Round announcements happen (end summaries, next-round pings)
- Admin coordination happens (admin-only channels)
- Moderation actions are discussed + recorded
- Bans are appealed
- Community events are planned

Discord also acts as an identity / verification backbone — many SS14
communities tie in-game admin status to Discord roles.

## Scope — phased rollout

### Phase 1: create the server + minimum viable channels
1. Create a new Discord server "Vacation Station 14" (owner: azigler).
2. Enable **Community** mode (unlocks announcement channels, screening).
3. Channel layout (start conservative; expand later):
   - `#welcome` — auto-sends new joiners the rules + connect info
   - `#announcements` — admin-only posts, everyone reads
   - `#rules` — formal ruleset (see vs-3e3); read-only for members
   - `#general` — main chat
   - `#off-topic` — low-stakes chat
   - `#bug-reports` — structured ticket intake (thread-per-report)
   - `#suggestions` — feature / content requests, upvotes via reactions
   - `#server-status` — webhook target for watchdog crash alerts + status
     transitions (private to @Admin; public mirror optional)
   - `#admin-only` — staff coordination
   - `#admin-logs` — every admin action (ban/kick/mute) mirrored here
     from the game server
   - `#appeals` — ban-appeal intake (private threads)
4. Roles (minimum):
   - `@Admin` — game-server admin powers; can moderate Discord too
   - `@Moderator` — Discord-only moderation (optional, smaller role)
   - `@Verified` — confirmed via Wizards Den OAuth (optional for now)
   - `@Everyone` — default
   - Pingable role `@Players` for opt-in announcement pings

### Phase 2: watchdog + game-server hooks
1. Create a Discord incoming webhook in `#server-status`.
2. Populate `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` `Notification.DiscordWebhook`
   with the URL. Restart watchdog. Crash + restart events now DM the channel.
3. Create a second webhook in `#admin-logs`.
4. Configure SS14 admin-log Discord export (cvar: `discord.logs_webhook`
   or similar — verify exact cvar name in CVars.cs). Every kick/ban posts.
5. Create a third webhook in `#announcements` for round-end summaries
   (uses SS14's round-end discord integration).

### Phase 3: community bot — Red-DiscordBot + wizard-cogs (as-is)

**Decision (2026-04-14, superseded the original custom-bot plan):**
Run Wizden's exact bot stack — Red-DiscordBot (Python 3.11+) with
`space-wizards/wizard-cogs` installed. No custom bot code; we adopt
the official cogs the same way we adopt SS14.Admin, SS14.MapServer,
ss14-cookbook, and RMC14-document-simu — vanilla deploy, defer
forking until a real VS14 divergence demands it. Pattern matches the
ancillary-services assessment from vs-19h.

Runs as its own systemd service on the host (shape mirrors
`ss14-admin` / `cookbook` (pattern-match ops/ layout)). In-repo footprint is a small
`ops/red-discordbot/` directory containing the systemd unit +
install script + `.env.example`; the bot itself is installed via
Red's downloader, not committed.

Cogs to load on first run (see Notes "Tier 3" for exact commands +
source URLs):
- `gameserverstatus` — live server status posted into `#server-status`
- `poweractions` — `[p]restartserver` / `[p]stopserver` /
  `[p]updateserver` against our watchdog admin API at
  `https://ss14.zig.computer/watchdog/`. Gated to @Admin role.
- `echo` — admin announce helper
- `autoresponder` — optional, per vs-3e3 tone decision

Secrets (bot token, watchdog API token) in an `EnvironmentFile`
outside the repo, same pattern as SS14.Admin's OAuth env. Never
committed.

**Custom-bot / cog-fork is out of scope for Phase 3.** Revisit ONLY
if wizard-cogs genuinely can't cover a VS14-specific need. At that
point we'd fork wizard-cogs (not write a new bot from scratch) to
preserve upstream compatibility. File-on-demand per our
deploy-vanilla-defer-fork pattern. (The earlier vs-2bm bead, which
scoped such a port, was closed obsolete on 2026-04-16.)

### Phase 4: community automations
1. **Autorole**: give new joiners `@Everyone` + screening gate
2. **Automod**: built-in Discord automod for spam / slurs
3. **Reaction roles**: opt-in for pingable roles (e.g. `@Players` for
   round-start pings, `@EventNotify` for community events)
4. **Welcome DM**: auto-DM new joiners a rules-digest + connect info
5. **Verification via Wizards Den OAuth**: when a player authenticates
   to our game server via Wizden, mark them `@Verified` in Discord.
   Requires the community bot + game-side hook.
6. **Ticket system**: Tickets for ban appeals, complex bug reports,
   admin concerns. Built-in thread-based OR use a Discord add-on like
   Ticket Tool.

### Phase 5: integration polish
1. **Single source of truth**: rules / MOTD / positioning in
   `docs/community/rules.md`, `docs/community/motd.md`. Bot + website +
   game server all read the same files at build / deploy time.
2. **Discord → game chat bridge** (optional, opinionated).
3. **Donations / Patreon**: Discord role sync if we stand up a donation
   platform. Not urgent.
4. **Admin panel**: private admin channel has a bot command to issue
   server-wide announcements (`/announce "Round starting in 5 minutes"`
   → hits the watchdog API → game MOTD banner).

## Repo layout (Phase 3 deployment)
Pattern-match `ops/ss14-admin/` + `ops/cookbook/`:
```
ops/red-discordbot/
├── install.sh                install Red + cogs + systemd unit
├── red-discordbot.service     systemd unit (EnvironmentFile-driven)
├── .env.example              TOKEN, watchdog API key, guild id
└── README.md                 operator notes, cog reload commands
```
Red-DiscordBot + wizard-cogs installed via their own package flow at
install time; not vendored into this repo.

## Dependencies
- **vs-2y8** (watchdog behind Caddy+HTTPS) — webhook URLs should be
  HTTPS once Caddy's up
- **vs-3e3** (content writing) — bot can't post rules until rules are
  written. Phases 1-2 don't block on content; Phase 3+ do.
- **vs-2dr** (website) — single-source-of-truth content in-repo implies
  the website exists to consume the same content

## Acceptance Criteria

**Phase 1 (standalone deliverable):**
- [ ] Discord server exists with the channel layout above
- [ ] Roles created, basic permissions set
- [ ] Community mode enabled

**Phase 2:**
- [ ] Watchdog crash-alert webhook posts to `#server-status`
- [ ] Kill a test round, confirm the post
- [ ] Admin-log webhook streaming kicks/bans to `#admin-logs`

**Phase 3:**
- [ ] `ops/red-discordbot/` exists with install script + systemd unit
- [ ] Red-DiscordBot + wizard-cogs running under systemd on the host
- [ ] `gameserverstatus` posting live VS14 status in `#server-status`
- [ ] `poweractions` admin-gated; `[p]restartserver` tested against our
      watchdog with a throwaway admin account
- [ ] `echo` loaded; `autoresponder` loaded or skipped per vs-3e3 tone

**Phase 4:**
- [ ] Reaction roles wired
- [ ] Welcome flow tested with a throwaway account
- [ ] Wizden OAuth verification working OR documented as "later"

**Phase 5:**
- [ ] Rules/MOTD are single-source-of-truth (one file → 3 consumers)
- [ ] `/announce` slash command wired to game MOTD

## Out of scope
- Bridging voice chat / in-game voice (not a thing SS14 supports)
- Full social network features (profiles, friend lists, etc. — use
  Discord's built-ins)
- Replacing Discord with a self-hosted chat (Matrix, etc.)

## Open questions
- Do we want the chat bridge (OOC ↔ Discord)? Worth-it only once we have
  active users.
- Verification via Wizden OAuth requires hooking into the launcher's auth
  flow — feasible but non-trivial. Punt to Phase 4 or later.
notes:
## Official SS14 Discord integration reference (packed 2026-04-14)

Authoritative guide:
https://docs.spacestation14.com/en/server-hosting/setting-up-discord-integration.html
Raw source (keep in sync if it moves):
https://github.com/space-wizards/docs/blob/master/src/en/server-hosting/setting-up-discord-integration.md

The docs split integrations into THREE independent tiers. Pick which
you need — they layer cleanly. Prereq for all: enable Discord
Developer Mode (Settings → Advanced → Developer Mode ON) so you can
right-click to copy IDs.

### Tier 1 — Webhook-only (simplest)
Game server posts to Discord channels via incoming webhooks.
Setup: generate a webhook per channel
(https://support.discord.com/hc/en-us/articles/228383668), paste the
URL into `config.toml` or via cvar. No bot account needed.

Cvars (verified against `Content.Shared/CCVar/CCVars.Discord.cs` +
`CCVars.Chat.Admin.cs`):

    [discord]
    ahelp_webhook               = "<url>"   # in-game ahelp relays
    round_update_webhook        = "<url>"   # round start/end
    round_end_role              = "<role-id>" # optional ping on end
    vote_webhook                = "<url>"   # round vote events
    votekick_webhook            = "<url>"   # votekick events
    on_call_webhook             = "<url>"   # admin-on-call paging
    on_call_ping                = "<role-id>"
    watchlist_connection_webhook= "<url>"   # watchlist-flagged connect
    watchlist_connection_buffer_time = 5.0  # seconds coalesce
    ahelp_footer_icon           = "<url>"   # branding
    ahelp_avatar                = "<url>"   # branding

Watchdog has its OWN webhook for crash/restart alerts, set in
`/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` under `Notification.Discord` —
distinct from the game-server cvars above. Two webhooks total: one
for watchdog status, one or more for in-game events.

### Tier 2 — Embedded Discord.Net bot (in-process)
The game server hosts its own bot via Discord.Net. Enables
bidirectional OOC + admin chat bridge. Zero separate process.

Bot creation (Discord dev portal:
https://discord.com/developers/applications):
1. Create application → "New Application"
2. Give it a name + avatar
3. Installation tab → Install Link = "None" (keeps bot private)
4. Bot tab → disable "Public Bot"
5. Bot tab → ENABLE "Server Members Intent" + "Message Content Intent"
6. OAuth2 tab → URL generator → scopes: `bot` → bot permissions:
   `Send Messages`, `Read Message History`. Copy URL, paste in
   browser, install to your guild.
7. Bot tab → "Reset Token" → copy the token (one-time view — save
   to a passwd vault immediately)

Cvars to wire the bot:

    [discord]
    token    = "<bot-token>"   # CONFIDENTIAL — gitignored config
    guild_id = <server-id>     # right-click your server → copy ID
    prefix   = "!"             # should be unique across your bots

    [ooc]
    discord_channel_id = <channel-id>        # OOC bridge target

    [admin]
    chat_discord_channel_id = <channel-id>   # admin-chat bridge target

Omit either channel id to relay only one direction. Bot needs Read
Messages + Send Messages in each configured channel.

**CRITICAL**: every game server gets its OWN Discord application /
token. Token reuse causes "tons of fun bugs" per upstream. If we
run a staging server later, second bot.

### Tier 3 — Standalone Red-DiscordBot + wizard-cogs
Separate Python bot process. Modular via cogs. This is what Wizden's
official "Overseer" bot runs. Requires Python 3.11+.

Repos:
- Red-DiscordBot: https://github.com/Cog-Creators/Red-DiscordBot
- wizard-cogs:    https://github.com/space-wizards/wizard-cogs
- Red docs:       https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/
- Getting started:https://docs.discord.red/en/stable/getting_started.html
- Red cog index:  https://index.discord.red/  (third-party; no support)

Install path (after following Red's install + `[p]` = your bot's
chosen prefix):

    [p]load downloader
    [p]repo add wizard-cogs https://github.com/space-wizards/wizard-cogs
    [p]cog install wizard-cogs gameserverstatus
    [p]load gameserverstatus
    [p]cog install wizard-cogs poweractions
    [p]load poweractions
    [p]cog install wizard-cogs echo
    [p]load echo
    [p]cog install wizard-cogs autoresponder
    [p]load autoresponder

Available wizard-cogs (full list at the repo):
- **gameserverstatus** — post + auto-update live server status in a
  channel. Commands: `[p]statuscfg addserver ss14 vs14 <address>`,
  `[p]statuscfg addwatch <server> <#channel>`.
- **poweractions** — talk to SS14.Watchdog admin API. Commands:
  `[p]poweractionscfg add` (form captures watchdog URL + ApiToken +
  server-id), `[p]restartserver`, `[p]restartnetwork`,
  `[p]stopserver`, `[p]updateserver`. Our watchdog is at
  `https://ss14.zig.computer/watchdog/` — server-id matches what's
  in its appsettings.yml.
- **echo** — repeats user input (moderation tooling).
- **autoresponder** — lore jokes ("based" → "based on what", etc.).

GitHub webhook is NOT ported to Red yet; use Discord's native
github integration via a webhook + GitHub webhook config:
https://gist.github.com/jagrosh/5b1761213e33fc5b54ec7f6379034a22

### Discord Rich Presence (launcher)
SS14 launcher can show "Playing on Vacation Station 14" with a
custom icon. Upstream keeps shared Rich Presence assets at:
https://github.com/space-wizards/asset-dump/tree/master/discord-app-assets
Cvars `discord.rich_main_icon_id` + `discord.rich_second_icon_id`
point at asset IDs registered on the Discord application. Only
needed if we register our own icon set; defaults work otherwise.

### VS14 execution plan (tie back to scope)

| Tier | VS14 decision | Cross-ref |
|---|---|---|
| Watchdog webhook | Phase 2 | already wired cvars-wise; needs URL |
| Game-server webhooks (ahelp, round) | Phase 2 | cvars above |
| Embedded OOC/admin bridge | Phase 2 | optional; needs dedicated bot token |
| Standalone Red + wizard-cogs | Phase 3 | adopted as-is (decided 2026-04-14) |
| Custom bun+discord.js bot | — | rejected; vs-2bm closed obsolete 2026-04-16 |

**Decision (2026-04-14):** adopt Red + wizard-cogs as-is (minimal-
friction match with Wizden's patterns). Fork into our own cogs only
when a VS14-specific need emerges that wizard-cogs can't handle.
Keeps maintenance low; upstream fixes flow in free. vs-2bm — which
scoped a port of wizard-cogs features into a custom bot — was closed
obsolete 2026-04-16 because no custom bot exists to port INTO.

### Secrets handling
- Webhook URLs — embed a secret; treat as credentials. Store in
  `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` (already gitignored) and in
  the game-server config under `instances/.../config.toml`
  (gitignored). NEVER commit.
- Bot tokens — one per tier-2 bot and one for the tier-3 Red bot.
  Pattern-match the SS14.Admin OAuth env file: tight-perm env file
  outside the repo, referenced via EnvironmentFile / systemd, never
  written to logs.

### Prereq Discord features to toggle in the guild
- Community mode ON (unlocks announcement channels, screening)
- Developer Mode ON (for copying IDs)
- Server Members Intent + Message Content Intent on the bot app
  (required for member events and content-based autoresponse)

### Cost-of-delay note
These integrations unlock: player-visible round pings, ahelp relay
to Discord (so admins can respond while AFK-in-game), crash alerts
to `#admin-log` for on-call, status channel for "is the server up"
without opening the launcher. All high-leverage; the bottleneck is
the human setup (Discord server creation, role topology, bot-app
registration) — engineering is ~1 hour once those exist.

title: ops: fix mapviewer build — dotnet CSC parallel-race CS0006
description:
## Context
First mapviewer build after ops/mapviewer/ scaffolding (vs-236)
landed with a dotnet CSC parallel-build race:

```
CSC : error CS0006: Metadata file '...Content.Shared.Database.dll'
      could not be found
CSC : error CS0006: Metadata file '...Robust.Shared.Maths.dll'
      could not be found
```

Content.MapRenderer depends on the Content.* + RobustToolbox
projects. `dotnet run -c Release` triggers a parallel restore +
build across ~30 projects; under concurrent compilation the
reference assemblies (`obj/Release/ref/*.dll`) sometimes get read
before the producing project finishes writing them.

## Fixes to try (in order)
1. `dotnet build -c Release -m:1` before `dotnet run` to serialize
   the project build order — then a plain `dotnet run --no-build`.
2. Alternatively, in `ops/mapviewer/build.sh`, replace the single
   `dotnet run --project Content.MapRenderer -c Release` with a
   two-step: build then run.
3. Cold-rebuild the full solution once to seed the reference
   assemblies, then incremental builds usually work.

## Reproduction
The game server (rebuilt by our Flavor A Phase 3 pipeline) uses
`env -i bash -c 'dotnet run --project Content.Packaging ...'` which
serializes differently and doesn't hit this race. mapviewer's
`dotnet run --project Content.MapRenderer` does.

## Status
- Build gets through the full scope of the 21k-file repo's dotnet
  restore + builds most projects; fails at the final link stage
  for specific projects whose ref assemblies aren't ready yet.
- `/maps/` on prod shows a "coming soon" stub.
- Not blocking anything else.

## Parent
vs-236 (mapviewer deploy)
notes:
## Maps LIVE at /maps/rendered/ (2026-04-13)

### What worked
- Content.MapRenderer inside the mapserver container: renders
  successfully. All 14 station-class maps produced clean WebP
  images (5-7K pixel sizes, multi-grid).
- Copied via `docker cp` from
  `/app/build/.../Resources/MapImages/`
  to `/var/www/vs14-maps/rendered/`.
- Nginx serves them at
  https://ss14.zig.computer/maps/rendered/<Name>/<Name>-N.webp

### What didn't work (root cause)
Content.MapRenderer exits **non-zero during its dispose/cleanup
phase**, AFTER the WebP files are written. MapServer treats
non-zero as build failure and discards the output (doesn't even
write the DB records or copy files out of /app/build).

Verified by running the same renderer command manually in the
container: "Processed 2 maps" → "automatic exit in a few
moments" → non-zero exit. Files exist. MapServer rejects.

### 14 maps rendered successfully
Bagel, Box, CentComm, Elkridge, Exo, Fland, Marathon, Oasis,
Packed, Plasma, Reach, Relic, Saltern, Snowball.

### 4 maps failed (not rendered)
arenas, debug, salvage, serpentcrest — likely fail to load in
the test-harness context. These are specialty maps (arenas +
debug = test maps; salvage = procgen; serpentcrest = niche).
Can investigate individually later if needed.

### Path forward — Path A (recommended)
Build our own one-shot docker runner in `ops/map-render/` that:
1. Runs Content.MapRenderer via `docker run --rm` on a fresh
   copy of the repo
2. Ignores exit code — checks if `Resources/MapImages/<Name>/*.webp`
   exists
3. Rsyncs successful renders to /var/www/vs14-maps/rendered/
4. systemd timer: weekly rebuild

Keeps MapServer running for the eventual GitHub-App path but
removes it from the critical path for /maps.

### Path B (future) — fix the non-zero exit
Either patch Content.MapRenderer (upstream PR) to exit clean, or
enable MapServer's GitHub App mode where each map is a separate
invocation (the non-zero exit still happens but only a single-map
failure at a time — not whole-batch discard).

### Close criteria revised
- [x] Container + orchestration running
- [x] Maps render successfully (14/18)
- [x] Maps served at /maps/rendered/*
- [ ] Path A automation committed + systemd timer

title: ops: prometheus + loki + grafana stack
description:
## Context
Level 5 observability stack: Prometheus for metrics, Loki for logs, Grafana for
visualization. SS14 server has native support for both (port 44880 exposes
Prometheus metrics, Serilog.Sinks.Loki pushes logs directly).

SS14.Watchdog also supports Loki push via its own Serilog config.

## Dependencies
- vs-h3u (watchdog) should be in progress or done (watchdog-level metrics/logs are valuable)
- vs-3ty (postgres) done (Grafana Connections panel queries Postgres)

## Task
Deploy Prometheus + Loki + Grafana via docker-compose on the same host as the
game server. Wire SS14 server and Watchdog to push metrics and logs.

## Acceptance Criteria

### docker-compose stack
- [ ] ops/docker-compose.yml in repo (or ops/observability/docker-compose.yml)
- [ ] Services: prometheus, loki, grafana (consider alertmanager later)
- [ ] Persistent volumes for each (survive container restart)
- [ ] Network isolation (stack internal, only Grafana exposed via Caddy)

### Prometheus
- [ ] ops/prometheus.yml scrape config:
  - job_name: "gameservers" (MUST match dashboard expectations)
  - Static target: host.docker.internal:44880 (or bridge network)
  - Labels: server: "vacation-station" (matches Loki name)
  - scrape_interval: 15s (docs say 1s, that's too aggressive)
- [ ] Prometheus UI reachable at localhost:9090
- [ ] Targets showing "UP" status
- [ ] Sample query `robust_player_count` returns data

### Loki
- [ ] ops/loki-config.yml with retention policy
- [ ] Loki reachable at localhost:3100
- [ ] Labels filter works: `{App="Robust.Server",Server="vacation-station"}`
- [ ] Retention configured (e.g. 30 days)

### SS14 server config
- [ ] instances/vacation-station/config.toml:
  [metrics]
  enabled = true
  host = "localhost"
  port = 44880
  [loki]
  enabled = true
  address = "http://localhost:3100"
  name = "vacation-station"   # MUST match Prometheus `server` label

### SS14.Watchdog config
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml Serilog Loki sink:
  - Address: http://localhost:3100
  - Name: vacation-station-watchdog (or similar)

### Grafana
- [ ] Reachable at localhost:3000 (fronted by Caddy for HTTPS)
- [ ] Admin password from password manager (not default)
- [ ] Datasources configured:
  - Prometheus: http://prometheus:9090
  - Loki: http://loki:3100
  - PostgreSQL (grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin): Vacation Station DB
- [ ] grafana-postgresql-datasource plugin installed

### Firewall
- [ ] Prometheus, Loki internal-only (not on public ports)
- [ ] Grafana behind Caddy with HTTPS (docs/NETWORKING.md pattern)
- [ ] Caddy reverse proxy: grafana.yourdomain.com -> localhost:3000

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md observability section
- [ ] Troubleshooting: no metrics showing, no logs showing
- [ ] Instructions for adding alerts later

title: harness: enhance C# static analysis in hooks and /lint
setup: Ubuntu dev environment research & script
ops: CONTRIBUTING.md rewrite + PR template + hygiene rules (vibe-maintainer supportive)
description:
## Context
Our `CONTRIBUTING.md` is currently inherited from Delta-V (itself
inherited from upstream SS14). After Flavor A Phase 1, we'll have
pure SS14's CONTRIBUTING.md — which describes SS14 upstream's
conventions and reviewer team, none of which apply to VS14.

We need our own CONTRIBUTING.md + PR infrastructure reflecting:
1. VS14's vibe-maintainer philosophy (vs-1ss): yes to AI-assisted
   PRs, we'll fix-merge rather than request changes
2. Our directory conventions (`_VS/`, `// VS` annotations)
3. The `:cl:` changelog convention (SS14 standard)
4. Bead-tracking expectation for non-trivial work
5. Solo-maintainer + AI assistance reality

## Task

### 1. Rewrite `CONTRIBUTING.md`
Sections:
- Welcome + VS14 positioning (vs-3e3 content feeds opening paragraph)
- Dev environment setup pointer → docs/DEVELOPMENT.md (nix-primary)
- Directory conventions: `_VS/` for new content, `// VS` inline
  annotations for upstream-file mods, `_DV/` / `_NF/` / etc. for
  cherry-picked features from sibling forks
- PR hygiene rules (from vs-1ss):
  - One concern per PR
  - Rebase before submission
  - Minimal file changes
  - No drafts (auto-closed by workflow)
  - `:cl:` block for player-facing changes
  - Bead ID reference for non-trivial changes
- **AI-assisted PRs: explicitly welcomed.** Most forks ban them;
  we don't. Contributors should flag AI assistance transparently
  in PR body, but it's not a gate.
- "We will fix-merge when possible": if your PR has a good core but
  rough edges, we'll likely absorb it and fix the rough edges
  ourselves, preserving your attribution in git + changelog
- Review expectations: median response target < 24h, fix-merge
  preferred over request-changes
- Code of conduct (short; reference CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)

### 2. `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`
Structured template that asks for:
- Summary (≤ 3 sentences)
- Bead ID (optional)
- `:cl:` block for player-facing changes
- Checklist: rebased / single-concern / minimal-changes / tests pass
- "AI-assisted?" checkbox (transparency only, not a gate)
- A reassuring line: "Don't fear submitting imperfect work. We'll
  absorb and fix when possible, crediting you in the changelog."

### 3. `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`
- `bug_report.md` — structured repro steps + environment
- `feature_request.md` — structured ask + pointer to bead workflow
- `config.yml` — blank issues disabled or redirected to Discord

### 4. AGPL contributor notice
One-sentence note in CONTRIBUTING: "Contributions are licensed
under AGPL-3.0. See LEGAL.md for details."

### 5. Cross-references
- docs/DEVELOPMENT.md gets a "Contributing" section linking here
- /vibe-maintainer skill is our companion to CONTRIBUTING.md for
  the maintainer side of the same relationship

## Acceptance
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md rewritten for VS14
- [ ] `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` in place
- [ ] `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` populated
- [ ] docs/DEVELOPMENT.md "Contributing" section
- [ ] AGPL contributor notice present
- [ ] Test PR confirms templates load properly in GitHub UI

## Dependencies
- Blocks on vs-ddu.2 (post-reset CONTRIBUTING.md replaced)
- Pairs with vs-1ss (/vibe-maintainer — the maintainer side)
- Pairs with vs-3e3 (content — welcome paragraph comes from there)

## Out of scope
- Formal CLA (AGPL + PR body is enough for our scale)
- Contributor onboarding bot automation (later if needed)
notes:
## Framing note
CONTRIBUTING.md needs to acknowledge explicitly that contributor
dynamics have shifted: AI assistance is welcomed (not hidden), PR
velocity is high, maintainer bandwidth is finite. The document sets
expectations both ways: contributors know we'll fix-merge and credit
them; maintainer knows what hygiene contributors are asked to meet.
This is not Delta-V-upstream's CONTRIBUTING.md with our name
stamped on; it's a fresh document reflecting our actual relationship
with the community.

title: ops: extend nix dev shell with ops validation tools
description:
## Context
`shell.nix` currently ships dotnet/graphics/python deps for building the game. Ops scripts and subagents still `apt-get install shellcheck` etc. mid-run because the flake doesn't expose ops validation tools. Pin them in nix so everyone gets the same versions, and subagents can drop the ad-hoc apt calls.

## Task
Add ops validation tools to the `dependencies` list in `shell.nix`. Scope: tools that validate committed artifacts (prometheus/loki configs, shell scripts, yaml, systemd units).

## Scope — add to dependencies
- `shellcheck` — shell script linter (used by setup.postgres.sh, setup.watchdog.sh, backup.sh, etc.)
- `yamllint` — YAML linter (ops/observability/*.yml, docker-compose.yml, appsettings.yml.example)
- `prometheus` — provides `promtool` for `promtool check config ops/observability/prometheus.yml`
- `grafana-loki` or `loki` (whichever package name nixpkgs uses) — provides `loki -verify-config` and `logcli`
- `grafana` — provides `grafana-cli` for datasource/dashboard validation
- `systemd` — provides `systemd-analyze verify` (may already be present on the host; adding anyway for reproducibility; confirm it works inside `nix develop` on Linux)
- `go-task` / `just` — **skip** unless you already see one used; don't add speculatively

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `shell.nix` `dependencies` list extended; keep existing entries intact
- [ ] `nix-instantiate --parse shell.nix > /dev/null` succeeds (syntax)
- [ ] `nix develop -c which shellcheck promtool yamllint loki grafana-cli` all resolve to /nix/store paths (run only if you have confirmed nix daemon is available in the worktree env; otherwise document the validation path taken)
- [ ] `flake.lock` regenerated if changed (shell.nix change shouldn't bump it unless you touched flake.nix)
- [ ] Update `CLAUDE.md` "Nix env in worktree subagents" section to note that these tools are now pinned — so subagents can rely on them being present without `apt-get install`
- [ ] One commit with `Bead: vs-2ti` trailer, gitmoji `:wrench:`

## Out of scope
- Do NOT add services-flake or process-compose (that's vs-1bb)
- Do NOT modify flake.nix unless absolutely required (shell.nix edit should suffice)
- Do NOT run `nix build` or heavy evaluations that could take minutes

## Notes for the subagent
- Check nixpkgs package names exact match: `grafana-loki` vs `loki` — on recent nixpkgs it's just `grafana-loki`. Use `nix search nixpkgs <name>` if unsure (may be slow/offline; if so, pick the name that matches the nixpkgs release-25.11 branch — you can WebFetch https://search.nixos.org/packages?channel=25.11&query=<name>).
- The shell.nix uses `pkgs` from a channel-pinned nixpkgs. Trust the channel's package naming.

title: ops: front ss14-watchdog admin api with caddy + https (lock down :5000)
description:
## Context
During vs-1np/vs-25k bring-up we had to bind the SS14.Watchdog admin API
publicly (`Urls: http://0.0.0.0:5000`) and open ufw port 5000 so the SS14
client could download `SS14.Client.zip` from the watchdog's Local update
provider. The provider always sets the cvar
`build.download_url=http://<BaseUrl>/instances/<instance>/binaries/SS14.Client.zip`
and the client needs to reach that URL.

Current state on zig-computer:
- `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` has
    - `BaseUrl: http://51.81.33.136:5000/`
    - `Urls: http://0.0.0.0:5000`
- `ufw allow 5000/tcp` (wide open)
- Watchdog admin endpoints (restart/update/stop/etc.) are reachable from
  the public internet, gated only by HTTP Basic auth with the generated
  ApiToken. A network observer between a client and the VPS can capture
  the token in plaintext.

This is fine for day-one testing but needs to close before anyone
important uses the server.

## Task

Front the watchdog with Caddy on HTTPS. Two target shapes — pick one:

### Option A: split domain
- `admin.vacation.example.com` → Caddy → `127.0.0.1:5000` (full watchdog API)
- `download.vacation.example.com/client.zip` → Caddy → `127.0.0.1:5000/instances/vacation-station/binaries/SS14.Client.zip` (public download only)
- Watchdog bind back to `127.0.0.1:5000`, ufw closes :5000 public

### Option B: one domain, path-based
- `vacation.example.com/client.zip` → Caddy → watchdog binaries endpoint
- `vacation.example.com/admin/*` → Caddy + basic auth passthrough → watchdog
- Same binding + ufw cleanup

Prefer A — cleaner ACL, less path rewriting. docs/NETWORKING.md already
has the pattern for Grafana; extend it.

## Changes required

1. **Pick a domain / subdomain** and DNS-point it at 51.81.33.136.
2. **Caddyfile** block(s) under `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile`:
   ```
   download.vacation.example.com {
       @clientzip path /client.zip
       handle @clientzip {
           reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:5000 {
               rewrite /instances/vacation-station/binaries/SS14.Client.zip
           }
       }
   }
   admin.vacation.example.com {
       basicauth {
           # optional extra layer if you want; watchdog does its own auth
       }
       reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:5000
   }
   ```
3. **Update watchdog appsettings.yml**:
   - `BaseUrl: https://download.vacation.example.com/` — what the game server
     advertises in `/info.download_url`
   - `Urls: http://127.0.0.1:5000` — bind back to loopback
4. **ufw**: `sudo ufw delete allow 5000/tcp` (Caddy 80/443 are public, watchdog isn't)
5. **Restart**:
   - `sudo systemctl reload caddy`
   - `sudo systemctl restart ss14-watchdog`
   - POST /instances/vacation-station/restart to force game server to pick up new download_url
6. **Verify**:
   - `curl -I https://download.vacation.example.com/client.zip` → 200 + valid TLS
   - `curl https://51.81.33.136:1212/info | jq .build.download_url` → matches the new HTTPS URL
   - `ss -tln | grep 5000` shows `127.0.0.1:5000` only (not 0.0.0.0)
   - Connect via SS14 launcher again, confirm still works
7. **docs/OPERATIONS.md**: add a "Watchdog behind Caddy" subsection
   alongside the existing Grafana Caddy docs.
8. **docs/NETWORKING.md**: add the download.vacation / admin.vacation blocks.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Watchdog bound to 127.0.0.1 only; ufw :5000 rule removed
- [ ] TLS cert valid for the chosen domain (Caddy auto-acme)
- [ ] Client download URL served over HTTPS
- [ ] ApiToken never crosses plaintext TCP again (any admin ops go via HTTPS)
- [ ] SS14 launcher connects + downloads client successfully
- [ ] docs updated (OPERATIONS + NETWORKING)
- [ ] record completion timestamp before close

## Out of scope
- Don't migrate UpdateType from Local to Manifest (separate bead — that's a
  real CI/CD change)
- Don't add OIDC / SSO in front of the watchdog — ApiToken alone is fine
  once the wire is encrypted
- Don't change the ApiToken itself (rotation is a separate runbook)

## Notes
- Caddy auto-acme requires ports 80 and 443 open on the cloud firewall
  (already required for any HTTPS setup).
- Watchdog's BaseUrl value is what ends up in the game server's `/info`
  response and ultimately in the client launcher's download request.
  Must match the Caddy-fronted public hostname exactly.
notes:
## Scaffolding committed (2026-04-12)

Repo-side work complete — see ops/caddy/. Actual deploy remains
user-gated:

### What's ready
- Caddyfile with Option A (split host: download + admin)
- install.sh script that ABORTS if placeholder not replaced
- Cutover procedure documented inline

### What needs you
1. Pick a domain (e.g. vacation-station-14.com)
2. DNS: download.<DOMAIN> + admin.<DOMAIN> → 51.81.33.136
3. Replace <DOMAIN> placeholder in ops/caddy/Caddyfile
4. Run ops/caddy/install.sh on prod
5. Update watchdog appsettings.yml + restart
6. Close ufw :5000

Leaving bead OPEN pending cutover. Once domain is picked, this
becomes a ~5-minute operation.

title: fix: vs-1bb dev services stack — prometheus config dup, postgres init order, grafana plugin noise
description:
## Context
Smoke-tested `nix run .#dev-services` for the first time. Stack evaluates and Loki+Grafana boot cleanly, but Prometheus and Postgres don't come up. All findings are in the vs-1bb dev flake definition — prod docker-compose is unaffected.

## Three bugs (two blocking, one cosmetic)

### Bug 1 (blocking) — Prometheus: duplicate `--config.file` flag
`flake.nix` passes `--config.file=${prometheusConfigDev}` via `extraFlags` to services-flake's prometheus module. The module already generates a `--config.file` arg internally, and Prometheus CLI parsing rejects the duplicate:
```
[prom1] Error parsing command line arguments: flag 'config.file' cannot be repeated
```

**Fix direction:** instead of extraFlags, feed the dev-overlay YAML to services-flake the "right" way:
- The services-flake prometheus module typically exposes an `extraConfig` option (raw YAML string merged into the generated config) or an `externalUrl`/`configFile` direct path. Read `/nix/store/.../services-flake/nix/services/prometheus.nix` to find the real interface.
- If the module has `configFile` as an option (not just extraFlags), point it at our dev-overlay derivation.
- If the module only offers `extraConfig` (string-merge), translate our `ops/observability/prometheus.yml` into the module's expected attrset form. May need to feed `global`, `scrape_configs`, `external_labels` as nix attrs rather than a raw file.
- Last resort: wrap `pkgs.prometheus` in a `writeShellScriptBin` that ignores the module's auto-generated flag and takes ours, then point `package` at the wrapper.

### Bug 2 (blocking) — Postgres: initialScript ordering
Log shows init runs `initialScript.before` (our SQL: `CREATE USER vs14 ...; ALTER DATABASE vacation_station OWNER TO vs14;`) BEFORE `initialDatabases` creates the `vacation_station` DB:
```
[pg1-init] CREATE ROLE
[pg1-init] ERROR:  database "vacation_station" does not exist
[pg1-init] STATEMENT:  ALTER DATABASE vacation_station OWNER TO vs14;
```
Whole service then teardown.

**Fix direction:**
- Move the `ALTER DATABASE ... OWNER TO vs14` to `initialScript.after` (runs after initialDatabases).
- OR set the owner at db-creation time via `initialDatabases = [{ name = "vacation_station"; }]` plus an extra `initialScript.after` that does the alter, OR use whatever richer syntax services-flake exposes for owner assignment.
- Check the module source for the exact hook names; `initialScript` may have `before`/`after` sub-attrs or it may be a single script where we should order the statements relative to initialDatabases ourselves.

### Bug 3 (cosmetic) — Grafana plugin install noise
Grafana 12.3.0 ships `grafana-lokiexplore-app` and `grafana-pyroscope-app` in its default auto-install list. On startup it tries to install them from grafana.com into `/nix/store/.../share/grafana/data/plugins/` which is read-only:
```
error="failed to extract plugin archive: could not create ..., permission denied"
```
Non-fatal — Grafana still serves. Just spam in the log.

**Fix direction:** disable the auto-install. Either:
- Set `services.grafana.graf1.extraConf.plugins.plugin_admin_enabled = false` or similar (read module options)
- Explicitly set the grafana option that controls which plugins auto-install to an empty list (look for `auto_updates`, `plugin_admin_enabled`, or `default_plugins` in grafana's ini settings)
- Override `GF_INSTALL_PLUGINS=""` if the module exposes env injection

## Validation
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` as usual.

1. `nix flake check --no-build` — still passes
2. Boot the stack in no-TUI mode:
   ```
   rm -rf .data
   nix run .#dev-services -- --tui=false > /tmp/vs14-stack.log 2>&1 &
   sleep 45
   ```
3. Probe health:
   ```
   curl -sS localhost:9090/-/healthy     # expect 200
   curl -sS localhost:3100/ready          # expect 200 "ready"
   curl -sS localhost:3000/api/health     # expect 200
   PGPASSWORD=dev-only-insecure psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c 'select 1'  # expect "1"
   ```
4. Confirm Grafana log no longer shows plugin-install permission errors
5. Kill stack: `pkill -f process-compose` then `rm -rf .data`

## Out of scope
- Do NOT add/remove services in the stack (keep just postgres/prom/loki/grafana)
- Do NOT change prod docker-compose / prometheus.yml / loki-config.yml / provisioning YAMLs — this is pure dev-flake fix
- Do NOT change shell.nix

## Notes for the subagent
- services-flake source on the current system (find via `find $(nix-store --realise $(nix eval --raw .#inputs.services-flake.outPath)) -name '*.nix'` or `nix eval --raw .#inputs.services-flake.outPath` then `ls` that path)
- Prometheus version in use: check `nix run .#dev-services -- --help` or the extraFlags log to confirm what we're passing against
- Postgres version is 17.7 per smoke test log

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `nix flake check --no-build` passes
- [ ] `curl localhost:9090/-/healthy` returns 200 after stack boot
- [ ] `psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c 'select 1'` connects and returns 1 (with PGPASSWORD=dev-only-insecure)
- [ ] `curl localhost:3100/ready` returns 200 (unchanged from current state)
- [ ] `curl localhost:3000/api/health` returns 200 (unchanged from current state)
- [ ] Grafana log shows no "Failed to install plugin" permission errors
- [ ] Single commit with `Bead: vs-2yf` trailer, `:bug:` gitmoji

## Commit example
```
:bug: nix: fix dev services stack boot issues

- Prometheus: replace extraFlags --config.file hack with services-flake's
  native configFile/extraConfig interface; stops duplicate-flag rejection
- Postgres: move ALTER DATABASE OWNER into initialScript.after so the DB
  created by initialDatabases exists before we try to reassign it
- Grafana: disable auto-install of baked-in plugins (lokiexplore, pyroscope)
  that fail with permission-denied writing to the read-only nix-store
  package dir

Smoke-tested: all four endpoints return 200 / serve queries.

Bead: vs-2yf
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```
