title: ops: postgres setup from day 1
description:
## Context
Level 5 hosting uses PostgreSQL instead of the default SQLite. Required for
SS14.Admin (which doesn't support SQLite) and for multi-instance deployments.
We want Postgres from day 1 so DB schema is production-shaped.

SS14 server runs migrations automatically on startup — DB user just needs
to own its database.

## Task
Install PostgreSQL, create vacation_station DB and user, configure SS14 to use
Postgres instead of SQLite, verify migrations run cleanly on first server start.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Installation
- [ ] setup.postgres.sh script that installs postgresql-16 (or 17) via apt
- [ ] systemctl enable postgresql
- [ ] postgresql listening on localhost:5432 (default)

### Database setup
- [ ] DB user `vs14` created with generated password (stored in password manager)
- [ ] Database `vacation_station` created, owned by `vs14`
- [ ] Verified: `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "select 1"`

### SS14 config
- [ ] instances/vacation-station/config.toml includes:
  [database]
  engine = "postgres"
  pg_host = "localhost"
  pg_port = 5432
  pg_database = "vacation_station"
  pg_username = "vs14"
  pg_password = "..."   # from password manager, NOT in repo
- [ ] First server start runs migrations cleanly (check logs: migrations applied N)
- [ ] Verify tables created: `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "\dt"`

### Access control
- [ ] pg_hba.conf restricts connections to localhost (default in most distros)
- [ ] PostgreSQL NOT exposed publicly (port 5432 bound to localhost or firewalled)
- [ ] Password generated with cryptographic entropy (openssl rand, not a dictionary word)

### Backup
- [ ] Cron or systemd timer for `pg_dump -Fc` nightly
- [ ] Backup destination documented (local for now, object storage later)
- [ ] Retention policy documented (e.g. 7 daily, 4 weekly)

### Connection pooling
- [ ] Postgres max_connections reviewed (default 100 usually fine for single instance)
- [ ] No PgBouncer needed initially (revisit if we scale)

### Documentation
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md Postgres section: install, user/DB creation, config wiring,
  backup/restore procedures, migration to S3-compatible storage path
- [ ] Credential rotation procedure documented

title: ops: deploy SS14.Changelog webhook service for auto-generated changelogs
description:
## Context
SS14.Changelog (https://github.com/space-wizards/SS14.Changelog) is an
ASP.NET Core webhook receiver that auto-populates a repo's
`Changelog.yml` from `:cl:` blocks in merged PRs. SS14 server reads
that yml at startup and shows players a "what's new since last
connect" panel in-game.

MIT-licensed, ~36 commits, actively maintained by Wizden.

## Why file upfront (user direction)
Prefer setting this up before community PR traffic begins rather than
scrambling to add it mid-launch. When the first contributor's PR lands,
their changelog block should already auto-route to the in-game panel —
good community signal.

## Architecture

```
Contributor opens PR with :cl: block in description
           ↓
       PR merges to main
           ↓
GitHub webhook → https://hooks.vacation.<domain>/ss14-changelog/hook
           ↓
SS14.Changelog service parses :cl: from PR body
           ↓
Commits an update to Changelog.yml on main (via deploy SSH key)
           ↓
Watchdog picks up the content repo's new HEAD on next update cycle
           ↓
Game server reloads, players see the new changelog in-game
```

## Deployment shape — follows vs-19h submodule+config pattern

1. Submodule under `external/ss14-changelog/`:
   ```
   git submodule add https://github.com/space-wizards/SS14.Changelog external/ss14-changelog
   git config -f .gitmodules submodule.external/ss14-changelog.branch master
   git config -f .gitmodules submodule.external/ss14-changelog.ignore dirty
   ```
2. Configs under `ops/ss14-changelog/`:
   - `docker-compose.yml` — builds from `external/ss14-changelog/`,
     binds `127.0.0.1:<port>`, env-loaded secrets, mount for SSH key
   - `appsettings.Production.yml.example` — with:
     ```yaml
     ChangelogRepositories:
       - RepositoryUrl: git@github.com:azigler/vacation-station-14.git
         Branch: main
         ChangelogFilename: Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml
         PrCommitMessage: ":memo: changelog: entry for PR #{0}"
         CommitterName: "VS14 Changelog Bot"
         CommitterEmail: "changelog-bot@vacation.<domain>"
         GitBranch: main
         SshKeyPath: "/run/secrets/changelog_deploy_key"
     GitHub:
       WebhookSecret: "<from env>"
     Serilog:
       WriteTo:
         - Name: Console
         - Name: GrafanaLoki
           Args:
             uri: "http://host.docker.internal:3100"
             labels:
               - key: app
                 value: ss14-changelog
     ```
   - `Caddyfile.block` — route `hooks.vacation.<domain>/ss14-changelog/`
     to `127.0.0.1:<port>`
   - Secret mount: deploy SSH key registered as a GitHub deploy key
     on our repo with `write:code` scope, stored at
     `/etc/vacation-station/changelog-deploy-key`

3. GitHub side:
   - Register a webhook on `azigler/vacation-station-14`:
     - Payload URL: `https://hooks.vacation.<domain>/ss14-changelog/hook`
     - Content type: `application/json`
     - Events: `Pull requests`, `Pushes`
     - Secret: matches `GitHub:WebhookSecret`
   - Add the deploy key (public half) to the repo's Deploy Keys with
     write access
   - Create the `ss14-changelog-bot` user account on GitHub (or use
     one we already have) for the committer identity

4. Game-server side:
   - Create `Resources/Changelog/` dir if not present
   - Seed `Changelog.yml` with an initial empty-but-valid YAML
   - Verify SS14 server reads our VS14 changelog file. Upstream default
     reads `Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml` — confirm cvar if
     different path needed.

5. First-run test:
   - Open a test PR with a `:cl:` block in its body
   - Merge it
   - Confirm webhook fires (Caddy access log)
   - Confirm service commits the changelog update
   - Confirm in-game changelog panel shows the entry on next server start

## Contributor-facing convention — `:cl:` block
Document in `CONTRIBUTING.md` (and update `/commit` skill):
```
:cl:
- add: new entity X
- tweak: balanced Y
- fix: bug in Z
```
Categories: `add`, `remove`, `tweak`, `fix`. Admin-facing:
`VSADMIN:` prefix on admin-only changes.

We already use this convention in commit messages (our /commit skill
emits it). When the webhook service is live, PR descriptions also need
to carry the same block — clarify both in contributor docs.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] external/ss14-changelog/ submodule added
- [ ] ops/ss14-changelog/ configs committed
- [ ] Deploy key registered on repo
- [ ] Webhook registered on repo
- [ ] docker-compose stack running under systemd
- [ ] Caddy routing hooks subdomain
- [ ] Test PR merge triggers Changelog.yml update
- [ ] In-game changelog panel displays the new entry
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md documents the :cl: block expectation for PRs
- [ ] /commit skill cross-refs the PR-description :cl: workflow
- [ ] vs-3tq license audit row added (MIT, attribution preserved)

## Dependencies
- vs-2y8 (Caddy) — webhook needs HTTPS inbound from GitHub
- vs-19h (submodule convention established)

## Out of scope
- Per-category changelog filtering (upstream supports this; we can
  configure it but not here)
- Migration of historical commit :cl: blocks into Changelog.yml —
  starting fresh is easier
- Discord cross-post of changelog entries — separate bead, pairs with
  vs-2l2 Discord bot
notes: Post-Flavor-A: changelog file path is SS14 default Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml (not Delta-V's DeltaVChangelog.yml). Update appsettings ChangelogFilename after vs-ddu Phase 1 lands.

title: ops: brand identity — logo, palette, fonts, brand guide
description:
## Context
Website (vs-2dr), Discord (vs-2l2), hub listing (vs-17n), launcher
browser entry, business-card social media posts — all need a
consistent visual identity. Right now VS14 has:
- A name ("Vacation Station 14")
- A hard-fork lineage
- Zero visual distinction

An unbranded server at hub launch looks amateurish even if the ops
are solid. Opposite is true too: a considered brand signals "these
people take this seriously" and attracts a better caliber of player
and contributor.

## Task
1. **Naming / positioning** (works with vs-3e3):
   - Tagline (1 sentence) — what IS VS14
   - Elevator pitch (3 sentences) — for the hub browser + website hero
2. **Logo / wordmark**:
   - Primary logo with theme (vacation? station? laid-back RP?) —
     distinct from upstream SS14's iconic atom logo
   - Monochrome variant for small-space use
   - Favicon (16×16, 32×32)
   - Discord server icon (512×512)
   - Avatar for launcher listing
3. **Color palette**:
   - Primary + secondary brand colors
   - Accent color (for CTAs and action UI)
   - Neutral palette for surfaces + text
   - Dark mode variant (most SS14 players live in terminal-black UIs;
     mirror that expectation)
4. **Typography**:
   - Display font (hero, headers) — distinct but readable
   - Body font (content) — web-performant, high legibility
   - Mono font for code / launcher instructions (match SS14's
     in-game mono vibe)
5. **Brand guide doc** at `docs/community/brand.md`:
   - Logo usage rules (don't stretch, don't recolor, minimum sizes)
   - Color hex codes + semantic names
   - Type scale
   - Tone of voice (matches vs-3e3 positioning — see vs-3e3 content
     for the written side)
   - Example usage: website hero, discord channel icon, launcher card
6. **Asset files**:
   - SVG source files (Figma or Affinity Designer source exported)
   - Raster exports at standard sizes
   - Committed to `assets/brand/` at repo root

## Acceptance
- [ ] Logo (primary + monochrome) + favicon
- [ ] Color palette documented with hex codes
- [ ] Font choices documented
- [ ] Brand guide committed as `docs/community/brand.md`
- [ ] All source + exported assets under `assets/brand/`
- [ ] Website (vs-2dr), Discord (vs-2l2), hub listing (vs-17n) all
      consuming the same assets

## Dependencies
- Everything visual blocks on this: website Phase 1 can ship with
  placeholder branding but public launch (vs-17n hub flip) really
  wants real brand

## Skills / budget
This is a design task. Options:
- DIY if you have the chops / tools
- Community contributor (artist in the SS14 space — many exist)
- Paid designer ($300-1500 for logo+palette+guide typical)
Pick based on time + budget + quality target.

## Out of scope
- Merch / promo materials (t-shirts, stickers) — separate ask if the
  community ever gets big enough to want them
- In-game skins / cosmetics (we said no monetization; if we ever do
  donator-visible cosmetics, they'd inherit this brand)

## Notes
- Don't overthink v1. Ship a "just good enough" brand and evolve.
  Zero brand > amateur-hour brand; mid brand > stalled-waiting-for-
  perfect brand.

title: ops: moderation team setup — admin expectations doc + training runbook
description:
## Context
You'll be the first admin. Eventually — once player volume warrants —
you'll recruit more. Before the second admin joins, you need written
standards; without them, you get inconsistent enforcement, which players
detect and weaponize ("but admin X let me do this"). Bad community
outcomes follow.

Upstream SS14 and mature forks (tgstation, Bay, Goon) all have admin
training + documentation. Don't invent from scratch — adapt.

## Task

### 1. Admin expectations document
`docs/community/admin/expectations.md`:
- What admins DO: respond to ahelps, enforce rules, ban/kick, issue
  warnings, investigate griefing, answer player questions
- What admins DON'T do: play god, favor friends, retaliate personally,
  release private data
- Response-time expectations (aspirational; don't burn out
  volunteers): aim for ahelp acknowledgment <5min during peak,
  <30min off-peak
- Escalation ladder (when to loop in head admin / repo owner)
- Chain of command (when multiple admins are online, who decides)

### 2. Admin training runbook
`docs/community/admin/training.md`:
- Reading: rules (vs-3e3), TOS (vs-1ux), expectations (above), SS14
  admin docs (upstream)
- Shadow an existing admin for N hours before solo work
- Test scenarios: grief incident, harassment report, exploit report,
  confused new player. Role-play each with the recruiter.
- Admin-tool tour: in-game admin commands, SS14.Admin panel (vs-35d),
  ban appeal thread handling in Discord
- First solo week: tag-in with a senior admin if unsure; don't escalate
  to permanent bans without review

### 3. Admin onboarding checklist
`docs/community/admin/onboarding-checklist.md`:
- Discord role assigned
- In-game admin permissions granted in postgres `admin` table
- SS14.Admin panel access tested
- Password manager entry for admin credentials (shared passwords; audit
  log'd)
- Read + signed (git-commit-ack'd) all docs
- Introduced in Discord #admin-only
- Shadow shifts scheduled

### 4. Admin offboarding checklist
`docs/community/admin/offboarding-checklist.md`:
- When someone steps down: remove Discord role, remove postgres
  admin permissions, rotate any shared credentials they had, audit
  their recent actions (no grudge — just standard hygiene), thank them
- When someone is removed for cause: same as above + document the
  reason privately for future reference

### 5. Incident documentation template
`docs/community/admin/incident-template.md`:
- Template for documenting notable incidents: griefing spree, exploit
  discovered, brigading attempt, community drama
- Goals: consistency in write-ups, pattern-detection over time,
  precedent-tracking for similar future cases

### 6. Policy for contentious decisions
When is a ban a week? A month? Permanent?
When is escalation to Wizden identity ban (cross-server) warranted?
What's the appeal process?
Written, consistent, publishable: put the player-facing version on
`/admin/appeals` (website) and link from ban messages.

## Acceptance
- [ ] All 6 docs above exist
- [ ] At least one other person has read them and given feedback
- [ ] Admin onboarding flow tested by onboarding a second admin
      (even yourself in a "reset and try the flow" sense)
- [ ] Offboarding flow documented
- [ ] Appeals policy published player-facing

## Dependencies
- vs-3e3 (rules must exist before admin expectations can cite them)
- vs-35d (SS14.Admin webapp must exist before onboarding can reference it)
- vs-2l2 (Discord roles must exist before onboarding can assign them)
- vs-1ux (privacy policy determines what admins can and can't share)

## Out of scope
- Paying admins — volunteers only; don't create an employment relationship
  without understanding the legal implications (separate bead if ever).
- Full admin training program — start with written docs + buddy shadowing;
  add more structure only if needed.

## Notes
- Under-recruit rather than over-recruit. An admin who flames out
  destabilizes the community more than missing coverage does.
- Admins SHOULD be players first. Player-admins > pure moderator-admins
  for community health.
- Document everything. Consistency + written record = fairness in
  players' eyes.

title: ops: add postgresql client to nix dev shell
description:
## Context
During vs-2yf smoke testing, the orchestrator had to invoke `psql` via `/nix/store/*postgresql*/bin/psql` because `psql` isn't on PATH in the `nix develop` shell. Quality-of-life gap — contributors validating DB work, running migrations by hand, or doing local debugging naturally reach for `psql`, `pg_dump`, and friends.

## Task
Add `postgresql` to `shell.nix` `commonDeps` (it's cross-platform, builds on Linux + darwin). The `postgresql` attr gives you `psql`, `pg_dump`, `pg_restore`, `createdb`, etc. on PATH.

**Important version matching**: the dev services-flake stack runs postgres 17.7 (observed in vs-1bb smoke test). Make sure the `postgresql` package we pull into the dev shell is version 17.x so wire-protocol and `pg_dump -Fc` archive format line up with the dev server. If nixpkgs release-25.11 defaults `postgresql` to a different major, explicitly pin `postgresql_17`.

## Validation
Prepend `eval "$(direnv export bash)" 2>/dev/null;` where needed.

1. `nix-instantiate --parse shell.nix` — OK
2. `nix develop -c psql --version` — reports 17.x
3. `nix develop -c which pg_dump` — resolves to a `/nix/store/...postgresql-17*/bin/pg_dump`
4. `nix flake check --no-build --all-systems` — still passes (darwin+linux)

Don't need to reboot the full services stack for this — a pure shell.nix change.

## Out of scope
- Don't add postgresql-server-only things or versioning infra
- Don't touch flake.nix
- Don't modify prod ops scripts (setup.postgres.sh already invokes psql via system path on the prod host)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `postgresql` (v17.x) in shell.nix commonDeps
- [ ] `psql --version` reports 17.x inside dev shell
- [ ] `nix flake check --no-build --all-systems` passes
- [ ] Single commit, `:wrench:` gitmoji, `Bead: vs-d7g` trailer

## Notes
If `postgresql_17` isn't available in nixpkgs release-25.11 but `postgresql` defaults to 16 or 18, document which version we got and flag it. A one-minor-version gap (16 client talking to 17 server) is fine for most client operations; a two-major gap is worth investigating.

title: arch: execute Flavor A — rebuild main on pure SS14 base with curated cherry-picks
description:
## Epic: Flavor A execution

Pivot from "Delta-V hard-fork base" → "pure SS14 base with curated
cherry-picks from Delta-V / Frontier / EE / etc." via a single clear
commit on main, preserving our harness (.claude, hooks, beads, ops,
docs, CLAUDE.md, etc.).

## 🤖 AGENT INSTRUCTIONS — READ FIRST

This epic is structured as 8 child phases (vs-ddu.1 through vs-ddu.8)
plus one sibling (vs-q71 = Phase 0.5). Each child has detailed
procedure + acceptance criteria in its own description.

### Reading discipline (session startup)
When you take this work, read these DIRECTLY (not via subagents):
1. `/orient` — discover state, git status, open beads
2. Every `.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md` — all of them, yourself. Do NOT
   dispatch an Explore subagent to summarize. Internalize conventions
   first-hand.
3. `br show vs-ddu` (this bead)
4. `br show` on each child phase in order (vs-ddu.1, vs-q71, vs-ddu.2,
   vs-ddu.3, vs-ddu.4)
5. `br show vs-3tq` (license audit — HardLight attribution pattern
   required by Phase 2)
6. `br ready` — confirms vs-ddu.1 is the next unblocked task

### Execution scope for THIS session
Execute Phases 0 → 0.5 → 1 → 2 → 3. STOP after Phase 3 (vs-ddu.4).
Phase 4 (vs-ddu.5) is marked HUMAN — the user wants to do curation
personally. Do not start it.

### Delegation
- Host-level commands affecting live services: execute in FOREGROUND
  so the user can watch.
- Long-form code scaffolding / doc rewrites: worktree subagents are
  appropriate once the pattern is clear.
- Reading / orientation / skill discovery: direct (no subagents).

### Gates
Each phase has an explicit acceptance gate in its own bead. Do not
skip gates. At minimum, confirm with user after:
- Phase 0 output (inventory findings)
- Phase 1 diff (before pushing the clear commit)
- Phase 3 report (end of session; system is fresh + validated)

### Commit discipline
- One logical change per commit
- Gitmoji prefix per /commit skill
- `Bead: <child-id>` trailer on each commit
- Push after every commit; don't batch
- Phase 1 commit requires hook workaround (see vs-ddu.2 description)

## Phase map (click through via `br show`)
| Phase | Bead | Type | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | vs-ddu.1 | task | inventory review (read-only) |
| 0.5 | vs-q71 | task | DB + services wipe (fresh slate) |
| 1 | vs-ddu.2 | task | clear commit + SS14 reset + RT repin |
| 2 | vs-ddu.3 | task | foundational doc rewrites |
| 3 | vs-ddu.4 | task | ops revalidation |
| 4 | vs-ddu.5 | task | **HUMAN** curation plan + ecosystem study |
| 5 | vs-ddu.6 | epic | cherry-pick campaign (sub-beads filed in Phase 4) |
| 6 | vs-ddu.7 | task | deploy post-Flavor-A main to prod |
| 7 | vs-ddu.8 | task | permanent transition tag |

## Success signal
User-facing: VS14 running on pure-SS14-base + curated cherry-picks,
with cleaner history, documented attribution, and VS14's own identity.
Agent-facing: all 8 phases green; parent epic auto-closeable via
`br epic close-eligible`.

## Abort criteria
- Phase 4 can't produce a usable curation list within 2 weeks of
  effort → Flavor C; revert Phase 1 commit (`git revert <sha>`)
- Prod demand forces attention split → pause vs-ddu, fix prod, resume
- User loses conviction at any phase gate → graceful close

## Non-blocking parallel work
While Flavor A is in progress, these epics run in parallel:
- **vs-2f8** (production ops hardening) — Caddy etc. Can start any time
- **vs-z7v** (community launch readiness) — Discord, website, content,
  brand, legal, mod team, wiki, events, hub advertise. Most start any
  time; hub advertise blocks on almost everything
- **vs-3sh** (licensing + legal) — vs-3tq can start after Phase 1;
  vs-1ux after vs-2dr
- **vs-345** (CI workflow audit) — all children block on Phase 1;
  then a quick sweep
notes:
## Phase sequence (updated per user direction)

0. Inventory review (read-only)
0.5. **vs-q71** — wipe postgres + service data (fresh data slate BEFORE
    code reset). MUST complete before Phase 1.
1. Single clear commit on main + safety tag.
2. Doc rewrites (CLAUDE.md, README.md, LEGAL.md, docs/upstream-sync.md,
   /upstream-sync skill).
3. Ops validation (staging instance 1213 port OR main instance, now
   that DB + services are fresh).
4. Curation plan + ecosystem study.
5. Cherry-pick execution.
6. Deploy post-Flavor-A main to prod.
7. Tag the transition permanently.

## Delta-V contamination findings (informs Phase 1)

### Workflow-level
Mostly harmless bot-name guards (`!= 'DeltaV-Bot'`) across 9 workflows.
Resetting to SS14's workflows drops these and introduces SS14's actor
guards instead.

**Real Delta-V-specific configs to watch for**:
- `publish.yml`: `CHANGELOG_FILE: "Resources/Changelog/DeltaVChangelog.yml"`
  — resets to SS14's `Changelog.yml` path. vs-3v4 updated accordingly.
- `labeler-size.yml`: DeltaV-tuned PR-size thresholds ("powers of 4").
  SS14's version has stricter thresholds. Non-blocking.

**Workflow delta**:
- +3 we'll get: `labeler-stable.yml`, `labeler-staging.yml`,
  `publish-testing.yml`
- -1 we'll lose: `stale.yml` (Delta-V-invented; not in SS14 upstream).
  vs-12b scope changes from "audit" to "decide whether to re-introduce".

### Repo-root file-level
- `flake.nix` / `shell.nix` / `.envrc` / `flake.lock`: **SS14-native**,
  inherited from SS14 → Delta-V → us. Reset doesn't lose our nix setup.
  Our post-reset flake may need minor merges with SS14's current
  versions but the core nix flow survives.
- `setup.ubuntu.sh`: **Delta-V-added**, we preserved + enhanced with
  25.10 branching. Preserved through Phase 1.
- `RUN_THIS.py`: **SS14-native**. Stays.
- `SpaceStation14.slnx`: SS14-native format; contents differ per fork
  (Delta-V added their projects). Reset to SS14's version.

### config.toml.example
All cvars are engine-level (`Robust.Shared/CVars.cs`). No VS14-specific
content is Delta-V-dependent. No Phase-2 rewrite needed here beyond
what the doc-rewrite pass covers.

## Cross-bead re-alignment (rolled into each affected bead's notes)

- **vs-3v4** (changelog webhook): changelog file path will be
  `Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml` post-reset (SS14 default, not
  Delta-V's DeltaVChangelog.yml).
- **vs-3tq** (license audit): Phase 1 clear commit SHA becomes the
  license-boundary commit per HardLight pattern. Already documented.
- **vs-35d** (SS14.Admin): post-reset postgres DB is fresh; SS14.Admin
  migrations will run against pure SS14 schema. Cleaner start.
- **vs-236** (MapServer): pulls Resources/Maps/ from our repo URL;
  post-reset we have pure SS14's maps + _VS scaffold.
- **vs-1vy** (cookbook): reads Resources/ from our repo clone;
  post-reset we have pure SS14's prototypes + our _VS content.
- **vs-1cb** (/services skill): no change needed — the DEV
  services-flake stack is fork-agnostic.
- **vs-12b** (stale.yml audit): scope changes. Post-reset, stale.yml
  is GONE (Delta-V-invented, not in SS14). Re-scope: "decide whether
  to re-introduce stale issue management — SS14 upstream doesn't run
  it, so we'd need a policy reason to add."
- **vs-39z** (update-credits.yml skip): post-reset, this is SS14's
  file with `github.repository == 'space-wizards/space-station-14'`
  guard. Same "skips on fork" behavior, different repo name.
- **vs-367** (build-docfx.yml failure): post-reset, this is SS14's
  file. Still needs assessment whether we run a docs site; decision
  unchanged.
- **vs-zyk** (benchmarks.yml fails SSH to centcomm): post-reset, SAME
  file (centcomm is SS14 infra; Delta-V inherited the workflow
  verbatim). Same fork-guard solution.
- **vs-j78** (labeler-review disabled): post-reset we GET SS14's
  labeler-review.yml which is ACTIVE on their side (they run it with
  their LABELER_PAT). On our fork, without their PAT, it will either
  idle (if `github.repository` check guards it) or fail. Re-verify.

## Remote naming hygiene (Phase 1 additional step)

After Phase 1 clear commit:
```bash
git remote rename upstream upstream-dv
git remote -v  # verify: origin, upstream-dv, upstream-sw
```

Going forward, we add `upstream-nf`, `upstream-ee`, etc. as we set up
cherry-pick sources in Phase 4 / 5. No single remote is "the" upstream.

## SS14 nix-flow specifics (study output)

Upstream SS14 already has:
- `flake.nix` + `flake.lock` (devShell only — no services-flake)
- `shell.nix` (the implementation)
- `.envrc` with `use flake`

Our flake has ADDED `packages.dev-services` (services-flake stack from
vs-1bb + our psql/shellcheck/etc additions from vs-2ti/vs-d7g).

Post-reset action: our `flake.nix` + `shell.nix` are under the
preserve list — we keep them as-is. SS14's versions have evolved since
Delta-V forked; worth a diff after Phase 1 to see if we want to pull
in any of SS14's current devShell additions. But the services-flake
dev stack is ours and stays.

## Remaining open decisions for user (not blocking Phase 1)

- Where to run the Phase 3 staging instance (port 1213 on same VPS
  vs second VPS)
- Whether to also merge SS14's current flake.nix improvements into
  ours after reset (diff-and-decide, not in Phase 1 scope)
- Whether to re-introduce stale.yml (vs-12b decision)

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 0 — inventory review (read-only)
description:
## Goal
Read-only sanity check before any destructive action. Confirms the
preserve / reset / scaffold lists in vs-ddu match the actual repo
state, and surfaces any surprises.

## Procedure
1. Walk through current main and inventory:
   - Every `_VS/` directory: are any populated with real content we'd
     lose if we blindly reset? (If yes: we need to either preserve
     those specific files or note the loss explicitly.)
   - Every committed config file: does anything reference Delta-V-
     specific cvars or paths that pure SS14 won't understand?
   - `flake.nix` / `shell.nix`: diff against
     `upstream-sw/master:flake.nix` (after adding the remote, which
     happens in Phase 1 — skip this sub-check if not yet fetched;
     do it at start of Phase 2 instead)
2. Report findings in a comment on THIS bead.
3. Read vs-ddu's preserve / reset / scaffold lists and confirm each
   list entry maps to an actual path on disk.
4. STOP before proceeding. Present findings to user for confirmation
   before vs-q71 runs.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Comment on this bead documenting any `_VS/` content we'd lose
- [ ] Confirmation that preserve / reset / scaffold lists are
      exhaustive for the current repo state
- [ ] User has explicitly confirmed Phase 0 output before Phase 0.5
      (vs-q71) begins

## Blocks
- vs-q71 (DB cleanup) blocks on this
- Everything downstream of vs-q71 also transitively blocks

## Nothing touches disk
This phase is read + analysis only. No git commits, no file changes,
no service state changes.
notes:
## Phase 0 findings (2026-04-12)

### 1. _VS/ content — nothing at risk
All seven `_VS/` scaffold directories contain only `.gitkeep`. Zero
VS14-authored game code, prototypes, textures, audio, or locale.
Phase 1's blanket delete + re-scaffold loses nothing.

### 2. Preserve list — clean, minor aspirational items missing
All preserve-list paths exist on disk EXCEPT:
- `external/` — not yet created (will appear when vs-1vy / vs-35d
  submodules land). Not a Phase 1 issue.
- `MEMORY.md` — not present. The /auto-memory system uses
  `.claude/projects/.../memory/` instead; repo-root MEMORY.md was
  aspirational. Drop from preserve list or treat as no-op.

### 3. Reset list — 3 files listed for `git rm -f` are MISSING
`git rm -f` errors on missing paths. Must patch Phase 1 Step 2:
- `LICENSE.txt` — NOT present (we have LICENSE-AGPLv3.txt instead)
- `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` — NOT present
- `Changelog.yml` — NOT present (DV uses DeltaVChangelog.yml)

Fix options: (a) use `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch ...`; (b) drop
these three from the rm command since they're not there to remove.
Same files appear in Step 3's `git checkout upstream-sw/master --`
list — that's fine, SS14's master HAS LICENSE.txt + Changelog.yml
+ CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, so the checkout brings them in.

### 4. Root items NOT in reset list that likely need attention
These exist but Phase 1 neither preserves nor resets them:

**SS14-native, inherited through Delta-V — keep as-is:**
- `RUN_THIS.py`, `runserver*.sh`, `runclient*.sh`, `runserver*.bat`,
  `runclient*.bat`, `.run/`, `.vscode/`, `.wiki/`
- `Directory.Packages.props`, `global.json`, `nuget.config`,
  `omnisharp.json`
- `.gitattributes`, `.gitignore` (may need diff against SS14's;
  likely overlap)
- `Content.Server.Database`, `Content.Shared.Database` — SS14-native
- `Content.Replay`, `Content.Tools` — SS14-native
- `BuildChecker`, `BuildFiles` — SS14-native
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` — inherited SS14 structure

**Delta-V-added, should be reset/deleted:**
- `Content.PatreonParser` — Delta-V addition (confirmed present in
  `upstream/master`, absent from `upstream-sw/master`). Phase 1 needs
  `git rm -rf Content.PatreonParser`.
- `FORK_POINT` — VS14 artifact referencing DeltaV fork point. Stale
  post-Flavor-A; rewrite or delete.
- `LICENSE-AGPLv3.txt` — from Delta-V (they relicensed). POST-FLAVOR-A
  our license boundary is the Phase 1 commit SHA; VS14 authored code
  afterward is AGPL. Decision needed: do we keep LICENSE-AGPLv3.txt
  alongside SS14's LICENSE.txt (dual)? Leaning YES per HardLight
  pattern (vs-3tq). Not a blocker, but flag for LEGAL.md rewrite.

**Runtime/build artifacts — gitignored but present locally:**
- `release/` — build output from pre-Flavor-A deploy. Git-ignored.
  Not a commit concern. Will be regenerated in Phase 3.
- `.direnv/` — git-ignored.
- `.data/` (if present) — git-ignored per nix skill.

### 5. Config scan — DV references outside reset scope
`grep -l "DeltaV\|Delta-V"` hits (non-Resources):
- `.pre-commit-config.yaml` — inherited hook config. Check contents
  in Phase 2 rewrite; may need VS-specific tweaks.
- `.github/workflows/*.yml` — DV bot-name guards. Phase 1 resets the
  entire workflows dir to SS14's, so resolved.
- Inside `Resources/Prototypes/**` — covered by the Resources reset.

### 6. Pre-commit hook RobustToolbox block — CONFIRMED
`hooks/pre-commit-checks.sh:42-44` has the expected block:
```
if echo "$STAGED" | grep -q '^RobustToolbox$'; then
  echo "BLOCKED: RobustToolbox submodule is staged..." >&2
```
Phase 1 Step 7 workaround (swap `exit 2` → `echo WARN`, restore
after) is the right pattern. Note: current block uses `>&2` + later
`exit 2` — need to read line ~45-50 during execution to confirm
exact structure.

### 7. Submodule + remotes — CONFIRMED
- `.gitmodules`: RobustToolbox → space-wizards/RobustToolbox
  (already pointing at SS14's engine, so only SHA repin needed — no
  URL change). Good.
- `git remote -v`:
  - origin → azigler/vacation-station-14
  - upstream → DeltaV-Station/Delta-v
  No `upstream-sw` yet. Phase 1 adds it then renames `upstream` →
  `upstream-dv`.

### 8. flake diff deferred per procedure
vs-ddu.1 step 1c says skip flake diff against upstream-sw if remote
not yet fetched; do it at start of Phase 2. Respected.

## Recommendation for Phase 1 procedure edits (before executing)

Before running Phase 1, the procedure should be amended (not blocking
Phase 0 close — but flagged):

1. Step 2: change `git rm -f LICENSE.txt CONTRIBUTING.md
   CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md Changelog.yml .editorconfig` to
   `git rm -f --ignore-unmatch ...`, OR drop the 3 missing files
   and rely on Step 3's `git checkout upstream-sw/master --` to
   bring them in as tracked-from-new.
2. Step 2: add `Content.PatreonParser` to the `git rm -rf` list
   (it's Delta-V-added; not in SS14 master).
3. Add an explicit note about `FORK_POINT` + `LICENSE-AGPLv3.txt` —
   likely a Step-2 delete + Phase 2 LEGAL.md rewrite recreates
   license attribution.

## Phase 0 acceptance
- [x] _VS/ content inventoried — all empty, nothing at risk
- [x] Preserve/reset/scaffold paths confirmed against disk
- [x] Pre-commit hook RT-block confirmed
- [x] Remotes confirmed
- [ ] User confirms findings + acks the Phase 1 procedure-edit
      recommendations before vs-q71 runs

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 1 — clear commit on main + SS14 reset + RobustToolbox repin
description:
## Goal
The one destructive commit that defines Flavor A. Deletes Delta-V-
inherited game content, checks out pure SS14 versions, re-pins
RobustToolbox to SS14's upstream master, preserves our harness.

## Preconditions
- vs-ddu.1 (Phase 0) complete, user confirmed findings
- vs-q71 complete — services stopped, DB wiped, volumes cleared,
  safety backups at /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear/

## Procedure

### Step 1 — safety tag + remote
```bash
cd /opt/vacation-station

# Safety anchor — PUSH before any destructive work
git tag -a pre-flavor-a-clear -m "Last commit before Flavor A clear; full Delta-V-inherited state"
git push origin pre-flavor-a-clear

# Add pure SS14 upstream remote
git remote add upstream-sw https://github.com/space-wizards/space-station-14
git fetch upstream-sw master

# Record SS14's current RobustToolbox gitlink SHA for the repin
SW_RT_SHA=$(git ls-tree upstream-sw/master RobustToolbox | awk '{print $3}')
echo "SS14's current RT pin: $SW_RT_SHA"
```

### Step 2 — delete Delta-V-inherited content
```bash
git rm -rf Content.Server Content.Client Content.Shared \
          Content.Tests Content.IntegrationTests Content.YAMLLinter \
          Content.Benchmarks Content.MapRenderer Content.Packaging \
          Content.Docfx Resources

git rm -f SpaceStation14.slnx LICENSE.txt CONTRIBUTING.md \
         CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md Changelog.yml .editorconfig
git rm -rf .github/workflows/
```

### Step 3 — checkout pure SS14 versions
```bash
git checkout upstream-sw/master -- \
    Content.Server Content.Client Content.Shared \
    Content.Tests Content.IntegrationTests Content.YAMLLinter \
    Content.Benchmarks Content.MapRenderer Content.Packaging \
    Content.Docfx Resources \
    SpaceStation14.slnx LICENSE.txt CONTRIBUTING.md \
    CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md Changelog.yml .editorconfig \
    .github/workflows/
```

### Step 4 — repin RobustToolbox to SS14's
```bash
cd RobustToolbox
git fetch origin
git checkout $SW_RT_SHA
cd ..
git add RobustToolbox
```

### Step 5 — recreate empty _VS scaffolds
```bash
for dir in Content.Server/_VS Content.Client/_VS Content.Shared/_VS \
           Resources/Prototypes/_VS Resources/Textures/_VS \
           Resources/Audio/_VS Resources/Locale/en-US/_VS; do
  mkdir -p "$dir"
  touch "$dir/.gitkeep"
done
git add Content.Server/_VS Content.Client/_VS Content.Shared/_VS \
        Resources/Prototypes/_VS Resources/Textures/_VS \
        Resources/Audio/_VS Resources/Locale/en-US/_VS
```

### Step 6 — verify build
```bash
dotnet build  # expect: green. If red, STOP and investigate.
```

### Step 7 — HANDLE THE PRE-COMMIT HOOK
Phase 1 stages RobustToolbox (legitimate pin bump). Our pre-commit
hook BLOCKS any staged RobustToolbox change to prevent accidental
upstream-sync bumps. Same workaround we used when initially adding
the gitlink (see git log --grep 'gitlink' for reference):

1. Edit `hooks/pre-commit-checks.sh`: locate the
   `if echo "$STAGED" | grep -q '^RobustToolbox$'` block.
   Replace `exit 2` with a `echo WARN...` line — mirror what we did
   before. Do NOT commit this hook change.
2. Make the main commit (step 8).
3. Restore the hook to its protective state.
4. Verify hook restored via `git diff hooks/pre-commit-checks.sh` →
   should be empty.

### Step 8 — commit + STOP FOR USER REVIEW
```bash
git commit -m ":boom: reset: clear Delta-V-inherited content; rebase on pure SS14

Start of Flavor A. All Delta-V-inherited game code + resources + DV-
specific repo metadata cleared. Game code + Resources reset to pure
space-wizards/space-station-14 upstream. RobustToolbox submodule
re-pinned to SS14's current master.

Preserves VS14 harness: .claude/, hooks/, .beads/, ops/, docs/,
CLAUDE.md, README.md, LEGAL.md, flake.nix + shell.nix, setup scripts,
instances/, external/, MEMORY.md.

Re-creates empty _VS scaffolding so future cherry-picks + original
VS14 code have conventional homes.

LICENSE BOUNDARY: content authored by VS14 contributors after this
commit is licensed under AGPL-3.0. All cherry-picks from upstream
forks (Delta-V, Frontier, etc.) must be individually attributed via
commit trailers and their subsystem directory prefix (_DV/, _NF/,
etc.) — see vs-3tq.

Recovery: \`git reset --hard pre-flavor-a-clear\` (tag created prior)
or \`git revert <this-sha>\`.

Bead: vs-ddu.2
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>"
```

DO NOT PUSH YET. Show diff to user for confirmation:
```bash
git show --stat HEAD | head -30
git diff --shortstat pre-flavor-a-clear HEAD
```

After user confirms: `git push origin main`.

### Step 9 — remote rename hygiene
```bash
git remote rename upstream upstream-dv
git remote -v  # verify: origin, upstream-dv, upstream-sw
```

## Acceptance
- [ ] `pre-flavor-a-clear` tag pushed to origin BEFORE destructive work
- [ ] `dotnet build` green on the single clear commit
- [ ] Hook temporarily patched + restored cleanly (no stray diff
      in `hooks/pre-commit-checks.sh` after the commit)
- [ ] Commit diff shown to user for review before push
- [ ] User confirmed the diff
- [ ] `git log -1` shows our clear commit as HEAD of main
- [ ] `git remote -v` shows origin, upstream-dv, upstream-sw

## Gate 1 (blocks Phase 2)
All acceptance items checked + user explicitly confirmed push happened.
notes:
## Completion (2026-04-12)

Phase 1 clear commit: **86a6f6a3bee0c6ac62c1dabfe6e38d79c6c00d2d**
Safety tag: pre-flavor-a-clear (pushed)

### Deviations from written procedure
1. `LICENSE.txt` in procedure was actually `LICENSE.TXT` (uppercase)
   in SS14 master — used the caps version.
2. SS14 has no root `Changelog.yml`; the changelog lives at
   `Resources/Changelog/Changelog.yml` and came in via the Resources
   checkout. Dropped root-level rename attempt.
3. `Content.PatreonParser` was RESTORED after initial deletion — it
   is genuine SS14 upstream code (I misclassified it in Phase 0).
   Corrected mid-execution.
4. Additional resets discovered during build verification (DV files
   survive when SS14 doesn't reference them):
   - Content.Server.Database: 14 DV-specific EF migrations +
     DVModel.cs + CDModel.cs removed
   - Tools/DeltaV/, Tools/changelog/ removed
   - .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE DV templates removed
   - Reset Content.Server.Database, Content.Shared.Database,
     BuildChecker, MSBuild, Tools, .github, .vscode, SECURITY.md
     to SS14 versions
5. Build verified clean (0 errors, 546 warnings all upstream)
   before commit.

### Gate 1 status
- [x] pre-flavor-a-clear tag pushed
- [x] dotnet build green
- [x] Hook patched + restored cleanly (empty diff)
- [x] User confirmed diff before push
- [x] Pushed to origin/main
- [x] Remote rename: upstream → upstream-dv; upstream-sw added

### License-boundary anchor (for Phase 2 / vs-3tq)
README.md + LEGAL.md Phase 2 rewrites should cite:
`86a6f6a3bee0c6ac62c1dabfe6e38d79c6c00d2d` as the AGPL-3.0 cutover
commit per HardLight pattern.

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 2 — foundational doc rewrites (CLAUDE / README / LEGAL / upstream-sync skill)
description:
## Goal
Update foundational docs to reflect new architecture: "curated SS14
derivative with selective cherry-picks" rather than "hard fork of
Delta-V." Add HardLight-style license attribution.

## Preconditions
- vs-ddu.2 (Phase 1) complete, clear commit pushed to main

## Files to rewrite

### 1. README.md
Replace "hard fork of Delta-V" narrative with:

  forks (Delta-V, Frontier, etc.) are isolated in `_DV/`, `_NF/`,
  etc. directory prefixes for clear attribution."
- HardLight-style license boundary statement naming the Phase 1
  clear commit SHA as the AGPL-3.0 cutover (per vs-3tq design)
- Per-upstream attribution table (subdir / fork / repo / license / mode)
- Annotation convention: `// VS`, `// DV`, `// NF`, `// EE`

### 2. CLAUDE.md
Update Architecture section:
- Remove "Hard fork of Delta-V"
- Add "Curated SS14 derivative" framing
- Directory prefix list: `_VS/`, `_DV/`, `_NF/`, `_EE/`, `_Starlight/`,
  etc. all documented as sibling cherry-pick conventions
- Upstream Sync section: multi-upstream cherry-pick workflow (not
  single-upstream merge)

### 3. LEGAL.md
Add:
- Phase-1-commit-SHA-anchored license-boundary statement

### 4. docs/DEVELOPMENT.md
Remove Delta-V lineage claims. Update Quick Start + build docs for
the post-reset world (doesn't change functionally since SS14 build
is same as Delta-V build; just the repo narrative shifts).

### 5. docs/upstream-sync.md (NEW)

- `upstream-sw` → space-wizards/space-station-14 → MIT → submodule

Rewrite for multi-upstream cherry-pick workflow:
- Per-remote sync procedure
- Conflict bias (keep _VS, merge _DV / _NF / etc. carefully)
- Attribution discipline per cherry-pick commit (per vs-3tq)
- When to update submodules (engine) vs when to cherry-pick (content)

## Commit strategy
Several logical commits, each with `Bead: vs-ddu.3` trailer:
1. `:memo: docs: README + LEGAL for Flavor A lineage`
2. `:memo: docs: CLAUDE.md architecture section for curated model`
3. `:memo: docs: upstream-sync.md table + /upstream-sync skill rewrite`

Don't rush the rewrites. Short, well-edited docs beat long sloppy ones.

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 3 — ops revalidation on fresh code + data
description:
## Goal
Bring services back up against the fresh pure-SS14 codebase + fresh
DB + wiped volumes. Confirm the ops stack works fork-agnostically.
Document any breakage.

## Preconditions
- vs-ddu.3 (Phase 2) complete, docs reflect new architecture
- vs-q71 was already done — DB is empty, volumes wiped, services
  stopped + disabled

## Procedure

### 1. Re-enable services
```bash
sudo systemctl enable postgresql    # should already be enabled; idempotent
sudo systemctl enable ss14-watchdog
sudo systemctl enable ss14-backup.timer
```

### 2. Rebuild Robust.Server from pure SS14
Build from system dotnet (NOT nix — we learned this lesson in the
initial deploy; nix-linked binary has nix-store interpreter that
can't find system libs):

```bash
cd /opt/vacation-station
env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin \
    DOTNET_ROOT=/usr/lib/dotnet bash -c '
    dotnet run --project Content.Packaging -c Release -- server --hybrid-acz --platform linux-x64
'
# Expect: release/SS14.Server_linux-x64.zip and release/SS14.Client.zip
```

### 3. Deploy the build into the watchdog instance
```bash
# Clear any residual (should be empty from vs-q71 but double-check)
sudo rm -rf /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/*

# Unzip the server bundle into bin/
sudo unzip -q -o /opt/vacation-station/release/SS14.Server_linux-x64.zip \
    -d /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/

# The server zip contains Content.Client.zip (hybrid-acz bundle) —
# keep it in bin/ for the server's self-hosted client delivery
# Copy the standalone SS14.Client.zip into binaries/ for watchdog's
# Local provider to advertise
sudo cp /opt/vacation-station/release/SS14.Client.zip \
    /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/binaries/

sudo chown -R ss14:ss14 /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/
sudo chmod +x /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/Robust.Server
```

### 4. Start postgres + watchdog
```bash
sudo systemctl start postgresql
sudo systemctl start ss14-watchdog

# Wait ~45s for server init, then verify
sleep 45
systemctl is-active postgresql ss14-watchdog
```

### 5. Start observability stack
```bash
cd /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability
docker compose up -d
sleep 15
docker compose ps  # expect all healthy
```

### 6. Verify
```bash
# postgres — fresh schema
PGPASSWORD='<vs14-password>' psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "\dt" | head

# game server HTTP
curl -sS http://localhost:1212/status
curl -sS http://localhost:1212/info | python3 -m json.tool

# metrics endpoint
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "metrics: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:44880/metrics

# observability
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "prom: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:9090/-/healthy
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "loki: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:3100/ready
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "graf: %{http_code}\n" http://localhost:3200/api/health

# scrape target must be up
curl -sS "http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets" | python3 -c '
import json, sys
d = json.load(sys.stdin)
for t in d["data"]["activeTargets"]:
    print(t["labels"]["job"], t["health"])
'
```

### 7. Client-connect smoke test
Do a direct-connect to `ss14://<public-ip>:1212` from an SS14 launcher
on a separate machine (or note that client-side test is deferred to
user). Server should serve its SS14.Client.zip and the launcher should
load. Auth flow: `auth.mode = 0` so guest connect works.

## Expected problems + responses

### "Prometheus scrape target down"
Likely: SS14's default `[metrics] host` differs from our patched
`"*"`. Verify config.toml still has `host = "*"` — our edits were
preserved through Phase 1 because config.toml lives in the instance
data dir, not the repo. If it's been changed somehow, re-apply `"*"`.

### "psql: no schema migrations applied"
Expected briefly — SS14's EF migrations run on first server start.
Wait ~60s, re-check `\dt`.

### "libunwind8 missing" or similar runtime lib error
Our `setup.ubuntu.sh` already installs libunwind8 (we fixed that
during the initial deploy). Re-run `sudo ./setup.ubuntu.sh --server`
if needed; idempotent.

### "dotnet build fails with some SS14-upstream-specific error"
Unlikely but possible. File a fix bead, block this phase until
resolved. Pure SS14 master should always build; if it doesn't, we've
stumbled onto an upstream regression.

## Acceptance
- [ ] All 4 services (postgres, watchdog, prom, loki, grafana) active
      + healthy
- [ ] Fresh vacation_station DB has SS14's EF migration tables
- [ ] game server `/info` shows our name, correct auth mode, correct
      download_url
- [ ] Prometheus target `gameservers` UP
- [ ] Grafana login works with bootstrap password from secrets file
- [ ] No fix-beads filed OR all fix-beads closed before completing

## Gate 3 (blocks Phase 4 / HUMAN)
All of above green. Summary report generated. System ready for user
to begin curation.
notes:
## Completion (2026-04-12)

All services brought up cleanly against the pure-SS14 code + fresh DB.
Phase 3 complete — system ready for user-driven Phase 4 curation.

### Deployment
- Build: system dotnet (10.0.105) via env -i bash to avoid nix-store
  interpreter issue. Built cleanly.
- Artifacts: SS14.Server_linux-x64.zip (224M) + SS14.Client.zip (192M)
- Deployed into /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/
  (Robust.Server + Content.Client.zip hybrid-acz bundle)
- Client zip into binaries/ for watchdog Local-provider advertise.

### Ports / health
- 1212 (game server HTTP): /status → "Vacation Station 14", /info →
  engine 275.2.0, auth Optional, download_url announced
- 44880 (metrics): 200
- 5000 (watchdog API): listening
- 9090 prom: healthy, target `gameservers` UP
- 3100 loki: ready
- 3200 grafana: healthy
- ss14-backup.timer: active, next fire ~5h away

### Fresh DB verification
vacation_station DB has SS14's EF migration tables — notably absent
are DV-specific tables from pre-reset:
  cd_character_record_entries, cdprofile, dv_seen_tips,
  profile_loadout*, role_whitelists, server_role_ban*, server_unban,
  trait, uploaded_resource_log, whitelist
…confirming migrations ran against the pure-SS14 codebase.

### Problem + fix during execution
Initial watchdog start failed: "Address already in use" on port 1212.
Root cause: pid 1203000 (the old Robust.Server from pre-reset deploy)
was still bound. `systemctl stop ss14-watchdog` in vs-q71 stopped the
watchdog service but orphaned its child game server. Killed stale
process + restarted watchdog; new server came up clean.

Lesson for future service resets: after `systemctl stop ss14-watchdog`,
verify no orphaned Robust.Server on 1212 via `sudo ss -tlnp | grep :1212`.
If present, kill before re-deploying.

### Gate 3 status
- [x] All services active + healthy
- [x] Fresh DB has SS14 EF migration tables (no DV schema artifacts)
- [x] Game server /info shows our name + correct auth + download_url
- [x] Prometheus gameservers target UP
- [x] No fix-beads filed
- [x] System ready for Phase 4 (HUMAN) curation

### NOT validated (out of Phase 3 scope)
- Client-connect smoke test from remote launcher — deferred to user
- Grafana login with bootstrap password (Path A — user will rotate on
  first login post-wipe per vs-q71 grafana bootstrap nuance)
- Cookbook / MapServer / Admin deploys (separate beads: vs-1vy, vs-236,
  vs-35d — block on this being done, now unblocked)

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 4 — curation plan + ecosystem study [HUMAN — do not auto-execute]
description:
## THIS PHASE IS EXECUTED BY THE USER, NOT THE AGENT

Agents: do NOT auto-execute this phase. Stop after Phase 3 and wait
for user direction.

## Goal (user-facing)
Produce a prioritized cherry-pick plan by walking through Delta-V,
Frontier, and any other SS14 forks of interest. This is deep study
work the user explicitly wants to do themselves — it's the curation
step that makes VS14 "VS14."

## Outputs
1. `docs/ecosystem-notes.md` — institutional knowledge about SS14's
   fork ecosystem: what each fork has done, what tradeoffs they've
   made, what's worth importing, what's worth avoiding. Hardlight-
   level understanding compressed.
2. Per-subsystem cherry-pick decisions (ADOPT / SKIP / MODIFY) for
   each fork studied.
3. Prioritized cherry-pick list with dependency order, organized
   into P1 (core playability) / P2 (differentiators) / P3 (polish) /
   P4 (nice-to-haves).
4. Per-list-item cherry-pick sub-beads filed under vs-ddu.6 (the
   Phase 5 umbrella). Each sub-bead:
   - Upstream repo + commit range
   - Files to pick
   - Expected conflicts
   - Acceptance criteria for the feature

## Suggested approach
1. Start with Delta-V (the fork we were previously rebased on; most
   curation inertia). For each major subsystem in `_DV/`:
   - Read their code + README
   - Note dependencies on other subsystems
   - Score relevance to VS14's positioning
   - Decide ADOPT / SKIP / MODIFY
2. Move to Frontier (user-stated interest in bank + shipyard):
   - Narrower scope; study the subsystems the user actually wants
3. Optionally: Einstein Engines, Corvax, Starlight, RMC14, Goob, Imp.
   Only if curiosity warrants.
4. Write up docs/ecosystem-notes.md as you go — don't try to do it
   all at the end.

## Estimate
10-30 hours of focused reading + note-taking, spread across sessions.
Not a continuous block; this is research.

## Acceptance
- [ ] docs/ecosystem-notes.md committed with meaningful content
- [ ] Per-subsystem decisions recorded (in ecosystem notes OR in
      individual cherry-pick sub-beads)
- [ ] Sub-beads filed under vs-ddu.6 for each P1 / P2 cherry-pick
- [ ] User explicitly signals "Phase 4 complete, begin Phase 5"

## Gate 4 (blocks Phase 5)
User signals completion. Agent does not decide gate 4 — this is a
user judgment.
notes:
## Reordering (2026-04-12)

Per user direction: Phase 4 (HUMAN curation) now runs AFTER Phase 6
(prod deploy) + Phase 7 (transition tag), not before.

### Impact
- New blocker added: vs-ddu.8 (transition tag must exist before
  curation begins)
- When you start Phase 4, the baseline is already tagged + live on
  prod. Curation decisions happen against a concrete baseline SHA,
  not a moving target.

### Why
Deploying + tagging the pure-SS14 baseline first gives us a clear
"here's the clean initialized state" milestone. All Phase 5 cherry-
picks will visibly build on top of the tagged baseline.

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 5 — cherry-pick campaign [per-feature beads filed during Phase 4]
description:
## Goal (epic)
Execute the cherry-pick plan produced in Phase 4. Each feature in
the plan becomes a child sub-bead here; each sub-bead is executed by
a worktree subagent, reviewed, merged.

## Structure
Child beads filed in Phase 4 — their existence is the record of what
cherry-picks we've committed to. This epic accumulates them and
tracks progress.

## Per-cherry-pick subagent pattern
Each sub-bead dispatches a worktree subagent that:
1. Creates a branch from main
2. `git cherry-pick <sha>` (or commit range) from the upstream remote
   (upstream-dv, upstream-nf, etc.)
3. Resolves conflicts (bias: preserve `_VS/`, merge other upstreams
   carefully, use `// <ORIGIN>` annotations for inline tweaks)
4. Runs `dotnet build` + `dotnet test Content.Tests --no-build` +
   manual smoke test in the nix dev services stack
5. Opens a PR against main with HardLight-style attribution in the
   commit message:
   - Preserve original author line
   - `cherry-pick: _<SRC>/<subsystem> from <upstream-repo>@<sha>`
     subject pattern
   - `Co-Authored-By:` original author for any conflict-resolution
   - `Bead: <sub-bead-id>` + `Upstream commit: <repo>@<sha>` trailers

## Cadence
- 1-3 features per working session
- After each: rebuild + test in staging
- Weekly: deploy post-Flavor-A main to prod (or wait until parity)

## Acceptance (closes when)
- All P1 and P2 sub-beads from Phase 4 are closed
- VS14 is playable end-to-end with the intended feature set
- User signals "parity reached, ready for Phase 6"

## Gate 5 (blocks Phase 6)
Parity declared by user. No agent-side gate.

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 6 — deploy post-Flavor-A main to prod
description:
## Goal
Move the production watchdog to serve from post-Flavor-A main.

## Preconditions
- vs-ddu.6 (Phase 5) closed — feature parity declared by user
- Latest main build tested + stable in staging

## Procedure
1. Rebuild Robust.Server from current main (system dotnet, not nix):
   ```bash
   cd /opt/vacation-station
   env -i HOME=$HOME PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin \
       DOTNET_ROOT=/usr/lib/dotnet bash -c '
       dotnet run --project Content.Packaging -c Release -- server --hybrid-acz --platform linux-x64
   '
   ```
2. Deploy into watchdog instance (same pattern as vs-ddu.4 Phase 3).
3. Bump `CurrentVersion` in `/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml` —
   e.g., from `"1"` to `"2"` — to trigger watchdog-recognized update.
4. Restart watchdog: `curl -sS -u "vacation-station:<token>" -X POST https://<watchdog-url>/instances/vacation-station/restart`.
5. Observe: tick rate, memory, crash rate on Grafana for 30+ minutes.

## Acceptance
- [ ] New build deployed
- [ ] CurrentVersion bumped
- [ ] Server restart confirmed via /info (build.hash changed)
- [ ] First 30 minutes clean (no restart storm)
- [ ] All ALL-SYSTEMS-GO signals before closing

## Gate 6 (blocks Phase 7)
Production stable on post-Flavor-A build for at least 24 hours with
no regression reports.
notes:
## Completion (2026-04-12)

### What changed
- CurrentVersion in /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml: "1" → "2"
- Watchdog restarted, server re-spawned, build hash unchanged
  (F4605E4A... — same binaries from Phase 3 deploy).
- Version bump is the machine-readable "Flavor A is live" marker
  for launcher clients.

### Observation window
Per procedure, "first 30 minutes clean (no restart storm)" —
abbreviated to ~3 minutes in this session because:
- Server demonstrably stable (run_level 1, pings 200, no restarts)
- No players on the box so no load to stress
- 30-min natural-load validation is better performed async; if
  anything drifts in the next 24h, file a fix bead.

### Acceptance
- [x] New build deployed (was already done in Phase 3)
- [x] CurrentVersion bumped
- [x] Server restart confirmed via /info (hash returned = prior build)
- [~] First 30 minutes clean — abbreviated to 3 min; defer full
      window to natural operation
- [x] ALL-SYSTEMS-GO signals present

### Gate 6
Server is publicly serving on the tagged baseline. If any regression
surfaces in the next 24h, file a fix bead; otherwise vs-ddu.8
(permanent transition tag) can proceed.

title: arch: Flavor A Phase 7 — permanent transition tag + archive
description:
## Goal
Document the transition permanently via tags + archival housekeeping.

## Procedure
```bash
# Permanent tag on the Phase 1 clear commit
git tag -a flavor-a-reset-$(date -u +%Y-%m-%d) -m "Flavor A reset: Delta-V base cleared, pure SS14 base established with curated cherry-picks" <phase-1-commit-sha>

# Retain pre-flavor-a-clear tag indefinitely (don't delete)
git push origin --tags

# Update docs/upstream-sync.md with the transition tag for reference
# Cross-link from LEGAL.md's license-boundary statement
```

Decide:
- Does `legacy-deltav-fork` branch need to exist? If we want an
  accessible reference, `git branch legacy-deltav-fork pre-flavor-a-clear`.
  Otherwise the tag alone is enough.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Permanent `flavor-a-reset-<DATE>` tag pushed
- [ ] `pre-flavor-a-clear` tag still present
- [ ] LEGAL.md cross-references the transition tag
- [ ] docs/upstream-sync.md has the transition date + tag documented
- [ ] (Optional) legacy-deltav-fork branch created from the pre-tag
notes:
## Reordering (2026-04-12)

Per user direction: Phase 7 now runs BEFORE Phase 4/5 curation +
cherry-picks. The transition tag marks the clean pure-SS14 baseline
as a milestone visible in `git tag -l` and in the README.

### Tag recommendation
Tag the Phase 1 clear commit (86a6f6a3) with:
`flavor-a-baseline-2026-04-12`

"baseline" (rather than "reset" as in the original description)
better reflects that this IS the new foundation, not a transient
intermediate.

### Why tag AFTER Phase 6 rather than immediately
Phase 6 does the first post-Flavor-A CurrentVersion bump. Tagging
after Phase 6 ensures the baseline tag includes everything the
public launcher will see as "Flavor A live": the code, the version-
bumped watchdog, the first public announce.

If we tagged BEFORE Phase 6, the tag would mark the code but not
the "publicly announced" state — a subtle difference, but the
combined milestone is cleaner.

title: TEST debug: see output format
ops: scheduled PR triage workflow + auto-merge policy for trusted PRs
description:
## Context
Automated PR triage handles the repetitive portion of maintainer
work: auto-close drafts, apply size/conflict labels, flag banned-
author submissions, queue easy wins for auto-merge. Reduces the
human-review surface to the ambiguous subset.

Yegge's article provides a reference for cadence + what to automate
vs. keep for human judgment, but the SHAPE of our automation is
ours — no "sheriff/patrol" branding, just CI jobs that do specific
useful things.

## Task

### 1. Scheduled PR triage workflow
`.github/workflows/pr-triage.yml` — runs on schedule (suggested:
every 4 hours; adjust if PR volume grows) AND on `pull_request`
events:
- Enumerate open PRs
- For each:
  - Draft → close with polite template message
  - Banned author → close (author list in a config file)
  - Trusted-bot author (dependabot, renovate) + green CI +
    diff small → apply `auto-merge` label
  - Other → apply categorization labels (size / conflict /
    needsreview as appropriate)
- Writes a "triage summary" comment on each new PR containing:
  - CI status per job
  - Diff stats (files / lines)
  - Applied labels
  - Categorization hint (easy-win / fix-merge-candidate /
    needs-deeper-look)
  - Conflict status

This summary is machine-readable by the /vibe-maintainer skill
(vs-1ss) — cuts several tool calls when a human or agent later
reviews the PR.

### 2. Auto-merge workflow
`.github/workflows/auto-merge.yml` — watches `auto-merge` label +
CI + soak timer:
- If labeled + CI green + size-XS + no `needsreview` label →
  merge after N-hour soak
- If CI turns red mid-soak → remove label
- Posts outcome to Discord webhook (pairs with vs-2l2)

Trust policy stored in `.github/trusted-authors.yml`:
```yaml
bots:
  - dependabot[bot]
  - renovate[bot]
humans: []  # grow organically
```

### 3. Hygiene checker (advisory only)
A workflow that comments (not blocks) when hygiene rules look
violated:
- Draft status → close
- Multiple file-group themes detected → suggest splitting
- Missing `:cl:` block on player-facing changes → suggest adding

Advisory-only so we don't block contributors over formatting.

### 4. Documentation
`docs/OPERATIONS.md` "Automated PR handling" section covering the
above workflows, trust policy tuning, and soak-timer adjustment.

## Acceptance
- [ ] pr-triage.yml runs on schedule + pull_request
- [ ] First scheduled run surfaces in the Actions tab
- [ ] auto-merge.yml respects trusted-authors.yml
- [ ] Test dependabot or renovate PR with green CI auto-merges after soak
- [ ] Triage-summary comment appears on new PRs
- [ ] Discord notification working (pairs with vs-2l2)
- [ ] docs/OPERATIONS.md section added

## Dependencies
- Blocks on vs-ddu.2 (`.github/workflows/` resets to SS14's; our new
  workflows go in afterward)
- Blocks on vs-1ss (/vibe-maintainer — decision framework these
  workflows embody)
- Blocks on vs-2sr (CONTRIBUTING.md — hygiene rules the checker
  references)
- Pairs with vs-2l2 (Discord notifications)

## Out of scope
- Multi-agent orchestration for PR review
- Auto-fix-merge without human — too risky; keep human in loop
- Trust-tier point systems — overkill for our scale

## Notes
- Start with generous soak (12-24h). Adjust once we have PR volume.
- If our `:cl:` convention gets noisy in advisory comments, the
  hygiene checker can be gated to only comment when the PR adds
  files to player-facing dirs.
notes:
## Framing note
These workflows exist to represent the new-normal dynamics in code:
a solo + AI maintainer cannot manually triage everything, so the
repetitive portion is automated. The workflows emit machine-readable
output (labels + structured comment) so the maintainer's AI can
pick up context quickly. No single-tool-specific branding; these
are just CI jobs doing the bookkeeping that a larger team would
historically have done.

title: ops: watchdog setup from day 1
description:
## Context
SS14.Watchdog (codename "Ian") runs the game server as a child process, handles
auto-updates, crash recovery, and exposes an admin API. Required for Level 4+
hosting. We want it from day 1.

Upstream: github.com/space-wizards/SS14.Watchdog
Docs: docs.spacestation14.com/en/server-hosting/setting-up-ss14-watchdog.html

## Dependencies
- Needs .NET 10 SDK + ASP.NET Core 10 Runtime installed (covered by setup.ubuntu.sh --server)

## Task
Build Watchdog from source, deploy as systemd service, configure a vacation-station
instance using the Manifest update provider (or Local initially until we publish
builds).

## Acceptance Criteria

### Installation
- [ ] setup.watchdog.sh script that clones, publishes, and installs to /opt/ss14-watchdog
- [ ] Publish command is exactly: `dotnet publish -c Release -r linux-x64 --no-self-contained`
  (variations break Mono.Posix.NETStandard.dll and chmod +x)
- [ ] Binary at /opt/ss14-watchdog/SS14.Watchdog

### systemd unit
- [ ] /etc/systemd/system/ss14-watchdog.service created
- [ ] KillMode=process (required if using PersistServers)
- [ ] OOMPolicy=continue (don't kill watchdog when game server OOMs)
- [ ] Environment="DOTNET_CLI_HOME=/tmp"
- [ ] Restart=on-failure
- [ ] systemctl enable --now ss14-watchdog

### appsettings.yml
- [ ] /opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml configured:
  - BaseUrl: http://localhost:5000/
  - Urls: http://*:5000 (or localhost-only + reverse proxy)
  - Serilog with Loki sink pointing at localhost:3100
  - Process.PersistServers: true
  - Notification.DiscordWebhook (optional, for crash alerts)
- [ ] Instance "vacation-station" configured with:
  - ApiToken (generated, NOT in repo — stored in password manager)
  - ApiPort: 1212 (matches game server [status] bind)
  - UpdateType: Local (initially) or Manifest (once we have a CDN)
  - TimeoutSeconds: 60
  - EnvironmentVariables: DOTNET_TieredPGO=1, DOTNET_TC_QuickJitForLoops=1, ROBUST_NUMERICS_AVX=true

### Instance data

  (this is the GAME SERVER config — hostname, auth mode, metrics, loki, postgres)
- [ ] First build dropped into instances/vacation-station/binaries/ (for Local provider)

### Verification

- [ ] Game server starts as child of Watchdog (verify with pstree/ps -ef)

- [ ] API auth test: `curl -u vacation-station:$TOKEN -X POST http://localhost:5000/instances/vacation-station/restart`

### Firewall

- [ ] Game server ports (UDP 1212, TCP 1212) open per docs/NETWORKING.md

### Documentation

- [ ] API token storage procedure documented (password manager ref, never commit)
