title: ci: decide fate of disabled labeler-review.yml
description:
## Context
`.github/workflows/labeler-review.yml` was commented-out end-to-end in the
initial fork (every line begins with `#`) but GitHub still registered it as
an active workflow and generated a zero-job "failure" run on every push to
main. During vs-25k we disabled it via
`gh api -X PUT ... /actions/workflows/<id>/disable` to match Delta-V
upstream's state (`disabled_manually`).

That's a stop-gap, not a decision. The file on disk still carries a
leftover stub with upstream's note: "The PAT does not have the correct
permissions to make this work; Eventually we'll want to replace this
workflow with a proper bot."

## Task — pick one path, do the work

### Option A: delete entirely
Cleanest. No ghost workflow, no confusion. Downside: every upstream-sync
may re-introduce the file, forcing us to re-delete. Add to the `/upstream-sync`
skill's "always drop" list to automate.

### Option B: revive with a proper PAT
Upstream's plan. Create a PAT with `contents:read` + `pull-requests:write`
in a bot account, store as `LABELER_PAT` secret, uncomment the file, update
the `github.repository` check to `azigler/vacation-station-14`, adjust the
team membership check (`space-wizards/maintainers` → our equivalent or
deleted).

### Option C: replace with a label-on-approval GitHub App
The "proper bot" the upstream comment aspires to. More involved — needs a
hosted app (or paid service like Probot). Only worth it if labeling is
actually part of our workflow. We don't currently use `S: Approved` for
anything, so likely not.

## Recommendation
**A** (delete) unless we commit to using approval labels in our review flow.
vs-???'s decision should answer: do we want `S: Approved` as a trigger for
anything (auto-merge, deploy gates, etc.)? If no → delete. If yes → B or C.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Decision made + recorded in this bead
- [ ] If delete: file removed, upstream-sync skill updated to drop on pull
- [ ] If revive: PAT provisioned, secret added, repo guard updated,
      workflow re-enabled via API
- [ ] The existing `disabled_manually` state is either resolved (delete)
      or flipped back to `active` (revive)
notes: Post-Flavor-A: SS14 upstream's labeler-review.yml is ACTIVE on their side. Without their LABELER_PAT secret on our fork, the workflow will try to run and either fail or no-op. Re-verify + re-apply disable-via-API after Phase 1 if we want the same quiet state.

title: ops: guidebook — render entity sprites from RSI files (not plain-text pills)
description: ## Context v1 of the guidebook render (vs-1e5) renders `` tags as plain-text pills showing the entity ID. The in-game guidebook renders the actual sprite. Players comparing web vs in-game see a degraded experience. ## Task Extend `ops/guidebook/render.py` to extract the sprite PNG for each embedded entity and render an `` in place of the pill. Entity sprite resolution: 1. Parse entity prototype YAML (Resources/Prototypes/**/*.yml) to build an entity-id → Sprite-component map. 2. The Sprite component references an RSI path (e.g. `Objects/Food/pasta.rsi`) + state (e.g. `carbonara`). RSI files live under Resources/Textures/. 3. Each RSI is a directory with a `meta.json` (lists states + dirs) + PNG files. The state name maps to the PNG file; for non-directional sprites use the first/default frame. 4. Copy the resolved PNG into the output tree at a stable path (e.g. `sprites/.png`) and emit ``. 5. Handle entity inheritance — if the entity doesn't declare a Sprite directly, walk `parent:` chain. 6. Handle composite sprites (layers) — for v2, use only the base layer; file a follow-up if full compositing needed. 7. Graceful fallback: if sprite can't be resolved, keep the text pill (don't crash the build). ## Acceptance - [ ] `` renders as an `` in the output HTML for at least 80% of embeds - [ ] Fallback to text pill when sprite can't be resolved (log warning) - [ ] Daily rebuild still completes in reasonable time (<2 min) - [ ] Output size is reasonable (de-dup sprites that appear on multiple pages) - [ ] Sprites visible in the live site after merge ## Notes - Python Pillow is already a likely install; prefer it over bespoke PNG slicing - RSI sprite sheets may need slicing — some sprites are combined into a single PNG with N states as sub-rects. Check meta.json `size` + `states[].delays` + the PNG dimensions - For simple non-directional states the PNG is usually already the right dimensions; check before reaching for Pillow - Reference implementation: Content.Client/Guidebook does this natively — study its resolution path ## Parent vs-z7v (community launch readiness)

title: research: adjacent SS14 services (watchdog, admin, cdn, etc)
ops: wipe postgres + service data for Flavor A — fresh slate before reset
description:
## Context
Phase 0.5 of vs-ddu (Flavor A reset). Before clearing Delta-V-inherited
game code from main, we want a fresh DATA slate too: blow away the
Delta-V-schema-migrated postgres DB, SS14 instance runtime data,
observability storage, and any build artifacts that might conflict
with the pure-SS14 build afterward.

Why sequence this FIRST: if we keep Delta-V's migrated DB, SS14's EF
migrations will see a DB with Delta-V-added tables / columns they
don't know about and either error or no-op past them. Pure SS14 server
should boot against a pure-SS14-migrated DB for a clean Phase 3 ops
validation. Zero data is worth losing — we haven't had any real
players yet.

## Order of operations

### 1. Stop all services cleanly
```bash
# Stop game server + watchdog
sudo systemctl stop ss14-watchdog

# Stop observability stack
docker compose -f /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml down

# Stop backup timer so it doesn't fire mid-wipe
sudo systemctl stop ss14-backup.timer
```

### 2. Final backup of current state
Safety: full pg_dump + tarball of /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/ +
archive of /var/backups/vacation-station. Timestamped. Stored outside
the cleanup targets so it survives.

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear
cd /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear

# Final DB dump (for reference; we don't plan to restore)
sudo -u postgres pg_dump -Fc vacation_station > pre-flavor-a.dump

# Tarball instance data
sudo tar czf instances-preclear.tgz /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/

# Tarball existing backups
sudo tar czf backups-preclear.tgz /var/backups/vacation-station/

# Record state summary
sudo -u postgres psql vacation_station -c "\dt" > db-tables-preclear.txt
sudo -u postgres psql vacation_station -c "\dn" > db-schemas-preclear.txt
```

### 3. Drop + recreate postgres DB
```bash
sudo -u postgres dropdb vacation_station
sudo -u postgres createdb --owner=vs14 vacation_station

# Verify empty
PGPASSWORD='<vs14-password>' psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "\dt"
# Expect: "No relations found."
```

The `vs14` Postgres role stays — same password, same permissions.
Only the DB contents reset.

### 4. Clear SS14 instance runtime data
```bash
sudo rm -rf /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/data/
sudo rm -rf /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/
sudo rm -rf /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/binaries/*
# NOTE: keeps config.toml + appsettings.yml — those are our setup, not data

# Recreate empty dirs
sudo mkdir -p /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/{data,bin,binaries}
sudo chown -R ss14:ss14 /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/
```

Keeping config.toml + appsettings.yml preserves generated ApiToken,
DB password reference, auth mode, hub/status/metrics cvars. Those
aren't "data" — they're our deployment's identity.

### 5. Wipe observability volumes
```bash
docker volume rm vs14-observability_prometheus-data \
                 vs14-observability_loki-data \
                 vs14-observability_grafana-data
# Next `docker compose up -d` recreates empty volumes + re-provisions
# datasources + dashboards from our committed config
```

### 6. Remove the pre-existing pg_dump artifacts
```bash
# Old backups belonged to the Delta-V-schema DB; no point keeping
sudo rm -f /var/backups/vacation-station/*.dump /var/backups/vacation-station/*.sha256
```
(The flavor-a-preclear backups from step 2 live in a DIFFERENT path;
those survive.)

### 7. Don't restart services yet
Services should stay stopped until AFTER vs-ddu Phase 1 (the clear
commit) AND we've rebuilt Robust.Server from the pure-SS14 tree.
Starting the watchdog against Delta-V-binaries on a fresh DB would
mean Delta-V's EF migrations run and we're back where we started.

Mark in this bead's completion note: "services are STOPPED; do not
start until vs-ddu Phase 3 test-run."

## Reversibility
Full rollback if we decide Flavor A isn't happening:
```bash
# Restore DB
sudo -u postgres dropdb vacation_station
sudo -u postgres createdb --owner=vs14 vacation_station
sudo -u postgres pg_restore -d vacation_station \
    /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear/pre-flavor-a.dump

# Restore instance data
sudo tar xzf /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear/instances-preclear.tgz -C /

# Restart services
sudo systemctl start ss14-watchdog
docker compose -f /opt/vacation-station/ops/observability/docker-compose.yml up -d
```

The flavor-a-preclear backup is small (~1MB DB since no player data +
~5KB configs) — retain it indefinitely.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Services cleanly stopped (no zombies)
- [ ] Backup artifacts present in /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear/
- [ ] `vacation_station` DB exists but contains zero tables
- [ ] `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/data/` empty
- [ ] `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/bin/` empty
- [ ] `/opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/binaries/` empty
- [ ] config.toml + appsettings.yml PRESERVED (ApiToken + PG password
      survive)
- [ ] Docker volumes removed: prometheus-data, loki-data, grafana-data
- [ ] Services left STOPPED (explicit — don't start until vs-ddu Phase 3)
- [ ] Completion note records the final state

## Dependencies
- Must complete BEFORE vs-ddu Phase 1 (code reset)

## Blocks
- vs-ddu Phase 3 (ops validation on fresh code+data slate)

## Notes
- vs-25z, vs-y6o, vs-1np, vs-25k are all CLOSED; this bead supersedes
  them for re-initialization under Flavor A. Those original beads
  captured the first-time setup work; reinit is a separate operation.
- We do NOT need to re-run setup.postgres.sh (postgresql package stays,
  vs14 role stays). Only DB contents reset.
- We do NOT need to re-run setup.watchdog.sh (binary stays; we just
  clear the RUNTIME data). After vs-ddu Phase 1, we'll rebuild a fresh
  Robust.Server from pure SS14 and drop it into bin/ — that's vs-ddu
  Phase 3.
- We do NOT need to re-run setup.ubuntu.sh (.NET, docker, ufw all
  stay).
notes:
## Completion (2026-04-12)

All steps executed cleanly. Notes on deviations / observations:

- Step 3 drop/recreate initially failed due to stale vs14 idle
  connection (pid 1203057, idle since 06:18:58 UTC). Resolved with
  pg_terminate_backend. Lesson: for future wipes, terminate all vs14
  sessions before dropdb.
- Step 4 preserved /opt/ss14-watchdog/instances/vacation-station/config.toml
  as specified. appsettings.yml lives at watchdog install root
  (/opt/ss14-watchdog/appsettings.yml), not per-instance, so it was
  untouched by the clear. Both files confirmed present post-wipe.
- Backup sizes: pre-flavor-a.dump = 146K (no player data as expected),
  instances-preclear.tgz = 876M (mostly build binaries from the
  initial deploy).
- Old weekly pg_dump artifacts at /var/backups/vacation-station/ were
  removed (Delta-V-schema; no restoration value post-Flavor-A).

Final state:
- Services STOPPED (watchdog, backup timer, observability stack)
- postgres running (needed to serve the fresh DB post-reset)
- DB vacation_station exists, 0 relations
- Instance data/bin/binaries dirs empty
- config.toml + appsettings.yml preserved (ApiToken, PG password,
  auth mode intact)
- Observability docker volumes removed (prom, loki, grafana data)
- Safety backups at /var/backups/flavor-a-preclear/ (876M total)

**Do not restart services until vs-ddu Phase 3 runs.**

title: ops: deploy RMC14-document-simu via submodule + VS14 paperwork repo
description:
## Context
Parent: **vs-19h** (decision matrix + submodule convention).

Deploy yagwog/RMC14-document-simu as-is via submodule at
`external/document-sim/`. Templates live in a separate github repo we
own (`azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork`). Only bring code into
the monorepo (`services/document-sim/`) if/when rebranding or
customization is needed.

## Deployment phases

### Phase A (start here): submodule + our templates
1. Create `github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork` with
   seed `templates/` dir + README.
2. Add submodule:
   ```
   git submodule add https://github.com/yagwog/RMC14-document-simu external/document-sim
   git config -f .gitmodules submodule.external/document-sim.branch main
   git config -f .gitmodules submodule.external/document-sim.ignore dirty
   ```
3. Inside the submodule, swap the templates source. RMC14 uses a git
   submodule too, but swap it or override via config:
   - If RMC14-document-simu's template submodule is configurable via
     env: set VS14 template path
   - If it's hardcoded: apply a small overlay script in
     `ops/document-sim/deploy.sh` that swaps the submodule target
     before building
4. `ops/document-sim/`:
   - `deploy.sh` — git submodule update, swap templates submodule to
     ours, build static output, rsync to served dir
   - `Caddyfile.block` — `paper.vacation.<domain>`
5. Seed one VS14 template as proof-of-concept.
6. `docs/OPERATIONS.md` section.
7. Update `docs/upstream-sync.md`.

### Phase B (when needed): GH-level fork
Triggered when: RMC14 branding is visible in UI, or we need code
tweaks upstream won't accept. Create `azigler/vacation-station-14-document-simu`
as a GitHub fork, point our submodule at that instead of upstream.

### Phase C (when needed): promote into monorepo
Triggered when: we're making substantial VS14-specific changes.
Follow the promotion procedure in vs-19h (git rm submodule, copy into
`services/document-sim/`, start editing).

## Acceptance (Phase A)
- [ ] `external/document-sim/` submodule pinned
- [ ] `azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork` exists with ≥1 template
- [ ] `ops/document-sim/` deploy script + Caddy block
- [ ] Serves at paper.vacation.<domain>
- [ ] At least one VS14 template renders

## Dependencies
- vs-2y8 (Caddy)
- vs-3e3 (content — template text is content)

## Out of scope
- Writing all templates (content bead)
- In-game paperwork integration (separate code-side feature bead)
notes:
## Scope update (2026-04-13)

Path decision: `/writer` under ss14.zig.computer (path parity with
/recipes, /maps, /watchdog, /admin). No subdomain. Updates Phase A
step 4's Caddyfile.block → nginx location block.

Paperwork repo: confirmed github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork
does not exist yet — needs creating (simple new repo, seed with
templates/ + README) per step 1.

Build stack: document-sim is node/vite-based. Nix shell.nix already
includes nodejs_20 + rsync (added with vs-1vy + vs-236).

### Revised delivery
1. Create github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork (empty
   + templates/.gitkeep + README)
2. Submodule add external/document-sim
3. Create ops/document-sim/ (build.sh, systemd unit + timer,
   install.sh pattern matching cookbook + mapviewer)
4. Update nginx vhost with `/writer/` → alias
   /var/www/vs14-writer/ (static output from build)
5. Update docs/upstream-sync.md + docs/OPERATIONS.md
6. Ready this bead for execution after user confirms paperwork
   repo created

### Blocker
- Creating github.com/azigler/vacation-station-14-paperwork (user
  needs to create or approve `gh repo create`)

### Sibling follow-ups already filed
- vs-1bp (cookbook build fix — /recipes/ not yet populated)

title: human: run vs-3ty postgres setup on host
description:
## Context
vs-3ty shipped the artifacts (setup.postgres.sh, backup units, config template, ops docs) but the orchestrator's worktree subagent is sandboxed and cannot touch the real host. This bead captures the human-only steps to actually bring Postgres up on the production box.

## Task — run on the target host
Execute the scripts from vs-3ty and verify each acceptance criterion. Expect to generate and safely store one credential.

## Steps
1. `sudo ./setup.postgres.sh` (or `PG_PASSWORD=<chosen> sudo -E ./setup.postgres.sh` if you want to bring your own password).
2. Capture the generated password. Store in password manager under "Vacation Station / postgres / vs14". NEVER commit.
3. Verify service: `systemctl is-active postgresql` → "active"; `ss -tln | grep 5432` → listening on 127.0.0.1.
4. Verify DB: `PGPASSWORD=<pw> psql -h localhost -U vs14 -d vacation_station -c "select 1"` returns 1.
5. Copy `instances/vacation-station/config.toml.example` → `config.toml`, fill in real password. Do NOT commit the filled version (add to `.gitignore` if not already).
6. Confirm pg_hba.conf is localhost-only (Ubuntu default). Confirm port 5432 is NOT exposed publicly (`sudo ufw status` or cloud firewall console).
7. Enable backup timer: `sudo systemctl daemon-reload && sudo systemctl enable --now ss14-backup.timer`. Confirm `systemctl list-timers | grep ss14-backup` shows next run.
8. Run a manual backup once to prime retention dir: `sudo /opt/vacation-station/ops/postgres/backup.sh`. Confirm output file appears in `/var/backups/vacation-station/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `systemctl is-active postgresql` → active
- [ ] `psql -U vs14 -d vacation_station` connects successfully
- [ ] `vs14` password stored in password manager (not in repo, not in shell history)
- [ ] `instances/vacation-station/config.toml` present on host with real password, gitignored
- [ ] Port 5432 not exposed to public internet (firewall-verified)
- [ ] `ss14-backup.timer` enabled + active; at least one backup file exists
- [ ] Record completion + timestamp in this bead before closing

## Notes
Report any deviations from setup.postgres.sh (e.g. postgresql-17 instead of 16, different install layout) so we can PR fixes back into the script.

title: ci: benchmarks.yml fails — SSHs to upstream centcomm
description:
## Context
`.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml` runs on schedule (daily `0 8 * * *`).
Latest run: 2026-04-12 08:19 UTC, **failure**. Single failing step: "Run
script on centcomm".

## Root cause
```yaml
- name: Run script on centcomm
  uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master
  with:
    host: centcomm.spacestation14.io
    username: robust-benchmark-runner
    key: ${{ secrets.CENTCOMM_ROBUST_BENCHMARK_RUNNER_KEY }}
```
`centcomm.spacestation14.io` is Space Wizards' internal benchmark host;
`CENTCOMM_ROBUST_BENCHMARK_RUNNER_KEY` is their private SSH key. Neither
exist for our fork. This workflow was **upstream-only from the start** —
it only ever worked for the space-wizards/space-station-14 repo.

## Options
### Option A: delete
We're not running upstream's benchmark infrastructure. Pure noise to keep.
Every daily cron generates a failure email / notification. Cleanest.

### Option B: guard with `if: github.repository ==`
Mirror what `update-credits.yml` does — skip on forks. Upstream-sync stays
quiet because the file is preserved; local cron shows "skipped" not
"failed". Slightly more merge-friendly than deletion.

### Option C: wire to our own infrastructure
Stand up a VS14-equivalent of centcomm for perf tracking. Large, not now.

## Recommendation
**B** — tiny change (one `if:` line), no merge conflicts on future
upstream-sync, benchmark infrastructure can be built later (would pair
with vs-2p3 / vs-13x's metrics pipeline).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `if: github.repository == 'space-wizards/space-station-14'` (or
      our own equivalent if we ever build it) added to the benchmark job
- [ ] Next scheduled run reports "skipped", not "failed"
- [ ] No notifications leak
notes: Post-Flavor-A: SAME file as Delta-V's (benchmarks.yml was inherited verbatim). Still SSHes to centcomm.spacestation14.io. Same fork-guard or delete solution applies.
